NAME

client.c - 👥 Per-client lifecycle manager: threading coordination, state management, and client lifecycle orchestration

#define CLIENT_DISPATCH_HANDLER_COUNT 13
#define CLIENT_DISPATCH_HASH(type)
#define MAX_AUDIO_BATCH 8

Variables


client_manager_t g_client_manager
Global client manager singleton - central coordination point. rwlock_t g_client_manager_rwlock = {0}
Reader-writer lock protecting the global client manager.

Detailed Description

👥 Per-client lifecycle manager: threading coordination, state management, and client lifecycle orchestration

1. Client connection establishment and initializationinitializationPer-client thread creation and management (receive, send, render))Client state management with thread-safe access patternspatternsClient disconnection handling and resource cleanupcleanupHash table management for O(1) client lookupslookupsIntegration point between main.c and other modules THREADING ARCHITECTURE PER CLIENT:

Each connected client spawns multiple dedicated threads:

  • Receive Thread: Handles incoming packets from client (calls protocol.c functions)
  • Send Thread: Manages outgoing packet delivery using packet queues
  • Video Render Thread: Generates ASCII frames at 60fps (render.c)
  • Audio Render Thread: Mixes audio streams at 172fps (render.c)

This per-client threading model provides several advantages:

  • Linear performance scaling (no shared bottlenecks)
  • Fault isolation (one client's issues don't affect others)
  • Simplified synchronization (each client owns its resources)
  • Real-time performance guarantees per client

DATA STRUCTURES AND SYNCHRONIZATION:

The module manages two primary data structures:

1.
client_manager_t (global singleton):
•
Array of client_info_t structs (backing storage)
•
Hash table for O(1) client_id -> client_info_t lookups
•
Protected by reader-writer lock (g_client_manager_rwlock)
•
Allows concurrent reads, exclusive writes
2.
client_info_t (per-client state):
•
Network connection details and capabilities
•
Thread handles and synchronization primitives
•
Media buffers (video/audio) and packet queues
•
Terminal capabilities and rendering preferences
•
Protected by per-client mutex (client_state_mutex)

Synchronization Patterns:

LOCK ORDERING PROTOCOL (prevents deadlocks):

1.
Always acquire g_client_manager_rwlock FIRST
2.
Then acquire per-client mutexes if needed
3.
Release in reverse order

SNAPSHOT PATTERN (reduces lock contention):

1.
Acquire mutex
2.
Copy needed state variables to local copies
3.
Release mutex immediately
4.
Process using local copies without locks

INTEGRATION WITH OTHER MODULES:

  • main.c: Calls add_client() and remove_client() from main loop
  • protocol.c: Functions called by client receive threads for packet processing
  • render.c: Render thread functions created per client
  • stream.c: Stream generation functions called by render threads
  • stats.c: Accesses client data for performance monitoring

THREAD LIFECYCLE MANAGEMENT:

Thread creation order (in add_client()):

1.
Initialize client data structures and mutexes
2.
Create send thread (for outgoing packet delivery)
3.
Create receive thread (for incoming packet processing)
4.
Create render threads (video + audio generation)

Thread termination order (in remove_client()):

1.
Set shutdown flags (causes threads to exit main loops)
2.
Join send thread (cleanest exit, no blocking I/O)
3.
Join receive thread (may be blocked on network I/O)
4.
Join render threads (computational work, clean exit)
5.
Clean up resources (queues, buffers, mutexes)

WHY THIS MODULAR DESIGN:

The original server.c contained all client management code inline, making it:

  • Hard to understand the client lifecycle
  • Difficult to modify threading behavior
  • Impossible to isolate client-related bugs
  • Challenging to add new client features

This modular approach provides:

  • Clear separation of client vs. server concerns
  • Easier testing of client management logic
  • Better code reuse and maintenance
  • Future extensibility for new client types

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

September 2025

Version

2.0 (Post-Modularization)

See also

main.c For overall server architecture

render.c For per-client rendering implementation

protocol.c For client packet processing

  server_ctx, socket_t socket, const char * client_ip, int port)

615                                                                                                           {
616   log_info("[TCP_ADD_CLIENT] ENTER: client_ip=%s, port=%d", client_ip, port);
617   // Find empty slot WITHOUT holding the global lock
618   // We'll re-verify under lock after allocations complete
619   int slot = -1;
620   int existing_count = 0;
621   for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
622     if (slot == -1 && g_client_manager.clients[i].client_id[0] == '\0') {
623       slot = i; // Take first available slot
624     }
625     if (g_client_manager.clients[i].client_id[0] != '\0' && atomic_load_bool(&g_client_manager.clients[i].active)) {
626       existing_count++;
627     }
628   }
629 
630   // Quick pre-check before expensive allocations
631   log_info("[TCP_DBG] SLOT_CHECK: existing_count=%d, max_clients=%d, slot=%d", existing_count, GET_OPTION(max_clients),
632            slot);
633   if (existing_count >= GET_OPTION(max_clients) || slot == -1) {
634     const char *reject_msg = "SERVER_FULL: Maximum client limit reached\n";
635     ssize_t send_result = socket_send(socket, reject_msg, strlen(reject_msg), 0);
636     if (send_result < 0) {
637       log_warn("Failed to send rejection message to client: %s", SAFE_STRERROR(errno));
638     }
639     return NULL;
640   }
641 
642   // NOW acquire the lock early for generating unique client name
643   log_info("[TCP_DBG] LOCK_ACQUIRE_START");
644   rwlock_wrlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
645   log_info("[TCP_DBG] LOCK_ACQUIRED");
646 
647   // Generate unique client ID (noun only, without counter or port)
648   char new_client_id[MAX_CLIENT_ID_LEN];
649   log_info("[TCP_DBG] GENERATE_ID_START");
650   if (generate_client_id(new_client_id, sizeof(new_client_id)) != 0) {
651     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
652     log_error("Failed to generate unique client ID");
653     return NULL;
654   }
655   log_info("[TCP_DBG] GENERATE_ID_DONE: id=%s", new_client_id);
656 
657   log_info("[TCP_DBG] LOCK_RELEASE_START");
658   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
659   log_info("[TCP_DBG] LOCK_RELEASED");
660 
661   // DO EXPENSIVE ALLOCATIONS OUTSIDE THE LOCK
662   // This prevents blocking frame processing during client initialization
663   log_info("[TCP_DBG] VIDEO_CREATE_START: About to create video frame buffer for client_id=%s", new_client_id);
664   video_frame_buffer_t *incoming_video_buffer = video_frame_buffer_create(new_client_id);
665   log_info("[TCP_DBG] VIDEO_CREATE_DONE: Video buffer creation returned %p", (void *)incoming_video_buffer);
666   if (!incoming_video_buffer) {
667     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create video buffer for client %s", new_client_id);
668     log_error("Failed to create video buffer for client %s", new_client_id);
669     return NULL;
670   }
671 
672   log_info("[TCP_DBG] VIDEO_DONE: About to create audio buffer");
673   audio_ring_buffer_t *incoming_audio_buffer = audio_ring_buffer_create_for_capture();
674   log_info("[TCP_DBG] AUDIO_DONE: Audio buffer created");
675   if (!incoming_audio_buffer) {
676     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create audio buffer for client %s", new_client_id);
677     log_error("Failed to create audio buffer for client %s", new_client_id);
678     video_frame_buffer_destroy(incoming_video_buffer);
679     return NULL;
680   }
681 
682   log_info("[TCP_DBG] AUDIO_QUEUE_START: Creating audio queue");
683   packet_queue_t *audio_queue = packet_queue_create_with_pools(500, 1000, false);
684   log_info("[TCP_DBG] AUDIO_QUEUE_DONE");
685   if (!audio_queue) {
686     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to create audio queue for client");
687     audio_ring_buffer_destroy(incoming_audio_buffer);
688     video_frame_buffer_destroy(incoming_video_buffer);
689     return NULL;
690   }
691 
692   log_info("[TCP_DBG] OUTGOING_VID_START: Creating outgoing video buffer");
693   video_frame_buffer_t *outgoing_video_buffer = video_frame_buffer_create(new_client_id);
694   log_info("[TCP_DBG] OUTGOING_VID_DONE");
695   if (!outgoing_video_buffer) {
696     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to create outgoing video buffer for client");
697     packet_queue_destroy(audio_queue);
698     audio_ring_buffer_destroy(incoming_audio_buffer);
699     video_frame_buffer_destroy(incoming_video_buffer);
700     return NULL;
701   }
702 
703   void *send_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC_ALIGNED(MAX_FRAME_BUFFER_SIZE, 64, void *);
704   if (!send_buffer) {
705     log_error("Failed to allocate send buffer for client %s", new_client_id);
706     video_frame_buffer_destroy(outgoing_video_buffer);
707     packet_queue_destroy(audio_queue);
708     audio_ring_buffer_destroy(incoming_audio_buffer);
709     video_frame_buffer_destroy(incoming_video_buffer);
710     return NULL;
711   }
712 
713   // NOW acquire the lock for the critical section: slot assignment + registration
714   log_info("[TCP_DBG] LOCK_START: About to acquire client manager write lock");
715   rwlock_wrlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
716   log_info("[TCP_DBG] LOCK_ACQUIRED: Client manager lock acquired");
717   log_info("[TCP_DBG] SLOT_RECHECK: About to check if slot is still available");
718 
719   // Re-check slot availability under lock (another thread might have taken it)
720   if (g_client_manager.clients[slot].client_id[0] != '\0') {
721     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
722     SAFE_FREE(send_buffer);
723     video_frame_buffer_destroy(outgoing_video_buffer);
724     packet_queue_destroy(audio_queue);
725     audio_ring_buffer_destroy(incoming_audio_buffer);
726     video_frame_buffer_destroy(incoming_video_buffer);
727 
728     const char *reject_msg = "SERVER_FULL: Slot reassigned, try again\n";
729     socket_send(socket, reject_msg, strlen(reject_msg), 0);
730     return NULL;
731   }
732 
733   // Now we have exclusive access to the slot - do the actual registration
734   client_info_t *client = &g_client_manager.clients[slot];
735   memset(client, 0, sizeof(client_info_t));
736 
737   // Initialize codec capabilities to zero (no codecs supported until CLIENT_CAPABILITIES received)
738   client->codec_capabilities_video = 0;
739   client->codec_capabilities_audio = 0;
740 
741   client->socket = socket;
742   client->is_tcp_client = true;
743   SAFE_STRNCPY(client->client_id, new_client_id, sizeof(client->client_id) - 1);
744   SAFE_STRNCPY(client->client_ip, client_ip, sizeof(client->client_ip) - 1);
745   client->port = port;
746   atomic_store_bool(&client->active, true);
747   client->server_ctx = server_ctx; // Store server context for cleanup
748   atomic_store_bool(&client->shutting_down, false);
749   atomic_store_u64(&client->last_rendered_grid_sources, 0);
750   atomic_store_u64(&client->last_sent_grid_sources, 0);
751   client->connected_at = time(NULL);
752 
753   memset(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx, 0, sizeof(client->crypto_handshake_ctx));
754   client->crypto_initialized = false;
755 
756   client->pending_packet_type = 0;
757   client->pending_packet_payload = NULL;
758   client->pending_packet_length = 0;
759 
760   // Assign pre-allocated buffers
761   client->incoming_video_buffer = incoming_video_buffer;
762   client->incoming_audio_buffer = incoming_audio_buffer;
763   client->audio_queue = audio_queue;
764   client->outgoing_video_buffer = outgoing_video_buffer;
765   client->send_buffer = send_buffer;
766   client->send_buffer_size = MAX_FRAME_BUFFER_SIZE;
767 
768   // Generate unique noun-based client name with transport type and port
769   // Note: We're holding the write lock here, so it's safe to iterate existing clients
770   log_info("[TCP_DBG] BEFORE_GENERATE_CLIENT_NAME");
771   if (generate_client_name(client->display_name, sizeof(client->display_name), g_client_manager.clients_by_id, port,
772                            true /* is_tcp */) != 0) {
773     // Fallback to numeric name if generation fails
774     safe_snprintf(client->display_name, sizeof(client->display_name), "client_%u (tcp:%d)", new_client_id, port);
775   }
776   log_info("[TCP_DBG] AFTER_GENERATE_CLIENT_NAME: display_name=%s", client->display_name);
777 
778   // Register client with named debug system using the generated name
779   (void)NAMED_REGISTER_CLIENT(client, client->display_name, NULL);
780 
781   log_info("Added new client %s from %s:%d (socket=%d, slot=%d)", new_client_id, client_ip, port, socket, slot);
782   log_debug("Client slot assigned: client_id=%s assigned to slot %d, socket=%d", new_client_id, slot, socket);
783 
784   // Register socket with tcp_server
785   asciichat_error_t reg_result = tcp_server_add_client(server_ctx->tcp_server, socket, client);
786   if (reg_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
787     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to register client socket with tcp_server");
788     log_error("Failed to register client %s socket with tcp_server", new_client_id);
789     // Don't unlock here - error_cleanup will do it
790     goto error_cleanup;
791   }
792 
793   g_client_manager.client_count++;
794   log_debug("Client count updated: now %d clients (added client_id=%s to slot %d)", g_client_manager.client_count,
795             new_client_id, slot);
796 
797   HASH_ADD_STR(g_client_manager.clients_by_id, client_id, client);
798   log_debug("Added client %s to uthash table", new_client_id);
799 
800   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
801 
802   // Configure socket OUTSIDE lock
803   configure_client_socket(socket, new_client_id);
804 
805   // Initialize mutexes OUTSIDE lock BEFORE registering them
806   // register_client_info_atomics needs these mutexes to be initialized first
807   if (mutex_init(&client->client_state_mutex, "client_state") != 0) {
808     log_error("Failed to initialize client state mutex for client %s", new_client_id);
809     remove_client(server_ctx, new_client_id);
810     return NULL;
811   }
812 
813   if (mutex_init(&client->send_mutex, "client_send") != 0) {
814     log_error("Failed to initialize send mutex for client %s", new_client_id);
815     remove_client(server_ctx, new_client_id);
816     return NULL;
817   }
818 
819   // Register all atomic fields for debug tracking - DISABLED to prevent deadlock
820   // register_client_info_atomics() and audio_ring_buffer_register_atomics() call
821   // NAMED_REGISTER which tries to acquire WRITE lock on named_registry.entries_lock
822   // The debug_sync thread holds READ lock while printing, causing reader-writer deadlock
823   // These are debug-only features not critical for functionality
824   // log_info("[TCP_DBG] BEFORE_REGISTER_CLIENT_ATOMICS");
825   // register_client_info_atomics(client);
826   // log_info("[TCP_DBG] AFTER_REGISTER_CLIENT_ATOMICS");
827 
828   // Audio buffer atomics registration disabled - same deadlock reason
829   // log_info("[TCP_DBG] AUDIO_BUFFER_REGISTER_ATOMICS_START: client=%p", (void *)client);
830   // if (client->incoming_audio_buffer) {
831   //   audio_ring_buffer_register_atomics(client->incoming_audio_buffer, new_client_id);
832   //   log_info("[TCP_DBG] AUDIO_BUFFER_REGISTER_ATOMICS_DONE");
833   // } else {
834   //   log_info("[TCP_DBG] AUDIO_BUFFER_REGISTER_ATOMICS_SKIPPED: incoming_audio_buffer is NULL");
835   // }
836 
837   // Register with audio mixer OUTSIDE lock
838   // CRITICAL: Do this BEFORE crypto handshake to avoid deadlock with mixer thread
839   log_info("[TCP_DBG] MIXER_ADD_SOURCE_START: g_audio_mixer=%p, client->incoming_audio_buffer=%p", (void *)g_audio_mixer,
840            (void *)client->incoming_audio_buffer);
841   if (g_audio_mixer && client->incoming_audio_buffer) {
842     log_info("[TCP_DBG] MIXER_ADD_SOURCE_CALLING: client_id=%s", new_client_id);
843     if (mixer_add_source(g_audio_mixer, new_client_id, client->incoming_audio_buffer) < 0) {
844       log_warn("Failed to add client %s to audio mixer", new_client_id);
845     } else {
846       log_info("[TCP_DBG] MIXER_ADD_SOURCE_SUCCESS: client_id=%s", new_client_id);
847 #ifdef DEBUG_AUDIO
848       log_debug("Added client %s to audio mixer", new_client_id);
849 #endif
850     }
851   } else {
852     log_info("[TCP_DBG] MIXER_ADD_SOURCE_SKIPPED: conditions not met");
853   }
854   log_info("[TCP_DBG] MIXER_ADD_SOURCE_DONE");
855 
856   // Create the client's persistent transport early (before handshake).
857   // It starts with NULL crypto; server_crypto_handshake() sets crypto_ctx after success.
858   client->transport = acip_tcp_transport_create(new_client_id, socket, NULL);
859   if (!client->transport) {
860     log_error("Failed to create ACIP transport for client %s", new_client_id);
861     if (remove_client(server_ctx, new_client_id) != 0) {
862       log_error("Failed to remove client after transport creation failure");
863     }
864     return NULL;
865   }
866   log_debug("Created ACIP transport for client %s (crypto pending handshake)", new_client_id);
867 
868   // Perform crypto handshake before starting threads.
869   // This ensures the handshake uses the socket directly without interference from receive thread.
870   // The handshake uses client->transport and sets its crypto_ctx on success.
871   log_info("[TCP_DBG] CRYPTO_INIT_START: About to call server_crypto_init()");
872   if (server_crypto_init() == 0) {
873     log_info("[TCP_DBG] CRYPTO_INIT_DONE: server_crypto_init() returned 0");
874     // Set timeout for crypto handshake to prevent indefinite blocking
875     // This prevents clients from connecting but never completing the handshake
876     const uint64_t HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_NS = 30ULL * NS_PER_SEC_INT;
877     asciichat_error_t timeout_result = set_socket_timeout(socket, HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_NS);
878     if (timeout_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
879       log_warn("Failed to set handshake timeout for client %s: %s", new_client_id,
880                asciichat_error_string(timeout_result));
881       // Continue anyway - timeout is a safety feature, not critical
882     }
883 
884     log_info("[TCP_DBG] SERVER_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_START: About to call server_crypto_handshake()");
885     int crypto_result = server_crypto_handshake(client);
886     log_info("[TCP_DBG] SERVER_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_DONE: result=%d", crypto_result);
887     if (crypto_result != 0) {
888       log_error("Crypto handshake failed for client %s: %s", new_client_id, network_error_string());
889       if (remove_client(server_ctx, new_client_id) != 0) {
890         log_error("Failed to remove client after crypto handshake failure");
891       }
892       return NULL;
893     }
894 
895     // Clear socket timeout after handshake completes successfully
896     // This allows normal operation without timeouts on data transfer
897     log_info("[TCP_DBG] CLEAR_SOCKET_TIMEOUT_START");
898     asciichat_error_t clear_timeout_result = set_socket_timeout(socket, 0);
899     if (clear_timeout_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
900       log_warn("Failed to clear handshake timeout for client %s: %s", new_client_id,
901                asciichat_error_string(clear_timeout_result));
902       // Continue anyway - we can still communicate even with timeout set
903     }
904 
905     log_debug("Crypto handshake completed successfully for client %s", new_client_id);
906 
907     // After handshake completes, the client immediately sends PACKET_TYPE_CLIENT_CAPABILITIES
908     // We must read and process this packet BEFORE starting the receive thread to avoid a race condition
909     // where the packet arrives but no thread is listening for it.
910     //
911     // SPECIAL CASE: If client used --no-encrypt, we already received this packet during the handshake
912     // attempt and stored it in pending_packet_*. Use that instead of receiving a new one.
913     packet_envelope_t envelope;
914     bool used_pending_packet = false;
915 
916     if (client->pending_packet_payload) {
917       // Client used --no-encrypt mode - use the packet we already received
918       log_info("Client %s using --no-encrypt mode - processing pending packet type %u", client->client_id,
919                client->pending_packet_type);
920       envelope.type = client->pending_packet_type;
921       envelope.data = client->pending_packet_payload;
922       envelope.len = client->pending_packet_length;
923       envelope.allocated_buffer = client->pending_packet_payload; // Will be freed below
924       envelope.allocated_size = client->pending_packet_length;
925       used_pending_packet = true;
926 
927       // Clear pending packet fields
928       client->pending_packet_type = 0;
929       client->pending_packet_payload = NULL;
930       client->pending_packet_length = 0;
931     } else {
932       // Normal encrypted mode - receive capabilities packet
933       log_debug("Waiting for initial capabilities packet from client %s", client->client_id);
934 
935       // Protect crypto context access with client state mutex
936       mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
937       const crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx = crypto_server_get_context(client->client_id);
938 
939       // Use per-client crypto state to determine enforcement
940       // At this point, handshake is complete, so crypto_initialized=true and handshake is ready
941       bool enforce_encryption = !GET_OPTION(no_encrypt) && client->crypto_initialized &&
942                                 crypto_handshake_is_ready(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx);
943 
944       packet_recv_result_t result = receive_packet_secure(socket, (void *)crypto_ctx, enforce_encryption, &envelope);
945       mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
946 
947       if (result != PACKET_RECV_SUCCESS) {
948         log_error("Failed to receive initial capabilities packet from client %s: result=%d", client->client_id, result);
949         if (envelope.allocated_buffer) {
950           buffer_pool_free(NULL, envelope.allocated_buffer, envelope.allocated_size);
951         }
952         if (remove_client(server_ctx, client->client_id) != 0) {
953           log_error("Failed to remove client after crypto handshake failure");
954         }
955         return NULL;
956       }
957     }
958 
959     if (envelope.type != PACKET_TYPE_CLIENT_CAPABILITIES) {
960       log_error("Expected PACKET_TYPE_CLIENT_CAPABILITIES but got packet type %d from client %s", envelope.type,
961                 client->client_id);
962       if (envelope.allocated_buffer) {
963         buffer_pool_free(NULL, envelope.allocated_buffer, envelope.allocated_size);
964       }
965       if (remove_client(server_ctx, client->client_id) != 0) {
966         log_error("Failed to remove client after crypto handshake failure");
967       }
968       return NULL;
969     }
970 
971     // Process the capabilities packet directly
972     log_debug("Processing initial capabilities packet from client %s (from %s)", client->client_id,
973               used_pending_packet ? "pending packet" : "network");
974     handle_client_capabilities_packet(client, envelope.data, envelope.len);
975 
976     // Free the packet data
977     if (envelope.allocated_buffer) {
978       buffer_pool_free(NULL, envelope.allocated_buffer, envelope.allocated_size);
979     }
980     log_debug("Successfully received and processed initial capabilities for client %s", client->client_id);
981   }
982 
983   // Start all client threads in the correct order (unified path for TCP and WebRTC)
984   // This creates: receive thread -> render threads -> send thread
985   // The render threads MUST be created before send thread to avoid the race condition
986   // where send thread reads empty frames before render thread generates the first real frame
987   const char *client_id_snapshot = client->client_id;
988   if (start_client_threads(server_ctx, client, true) != 0) {
989     log_error("Failed to start threads for TCP client %s", client_id_snapshot);
990     // Client is already in hash table - use remove_client for proper cleanup
991     remove_client(server_ctx, client_id_snapshot);
992     return NULL;
993   }
994   log_debug("Successfully created render threads for client %s", client_id_snapshot);
995 
996   // Register client with session_host (for discovery mode support)
997   if (server_ctx->session_host) {
998     client->session_client_id = session_host_add_client(server_ctx->session_host, socket, client_ip, port);
999     if (client->session_client_id == 0) {
1000       log_warn("Failed to register client %s with session_host", client_id_snapshot);
1001     } else {
1002       log_debug("Client %s registered with session_host as %u", client_id_snapshot, client->session_client_id);
1003     }
1004   }
1005 
1006   // Broadcast server state to ALL clients AFTER the new client is fully set up
1007   // This notifies all clients (including the new one) about the updated grid
1008   log_info("[TCP_DBG] BROADCAST_SERVER_STATE_START: About to broadcast to all clients");
1009   broadcast_server_state_to_all_clients();
1010   log_info("[TCP_DBG] BROADCAST_SERVER_STATE_DONE");
1011 
1012   // Register client atomics for debug sync inspection
1013   register_client_info_atomics(client);
1014 
1015   log_info("[TCP_DBG] ADD_CLIENT_RETURNING: client_id=%s, client=%p", client->client_id, (void *)client);
1016   return client;
1017 
1018 error_cleanup:
1019   // Clean up all partially allocated resources
1020   // NOTE: This label is reached when allocation or initialization fails BEFORE
1021   // the client is added to the hash table. Don't call remove_client() here.
1022   cleanup_client_all_buffers(client);
1023   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1024   return NULL;
1025 }

client_info_t * add_webrtc_client (server_context_t
  * server_ctx, acip_transport_t * transport, const char * client_ip,
  bool start_threads)


Register a WebRTC client with the server. Registers a client that
    connected via WebRTC data channel instead of TCP socket. This function
    reuses most of add_client() logic but skips:


    
  
  • Crypto handshake (already done via ACDS signaling)
    • 
  
  • Socket-specific configuration
    • 
  
  • TCP thread pool registration
    • 



DIFFERENCES FROM add_client():


    
  
  • Takes an already-created acip_transport_t* instead of socket
    • 
  
  • No crypto handshake (WebRTC signaling handled authentication)
    • 
  
  • No socket configuration (WebRTC handles buffering)
    • 
  
  • Uses generic thread spawning instead of tcp_server thread pool
    • 



Parameters


server_ctx Server context


transport WebRTC transport (already created and connected)


client_ip Client IP address for logging (may be empty for P2P)


Returns


Pointer to client_info_t on success, NULL on
  failure


Note


The transport must be fully initialized and ready to
  send/receive



Client capabilities are still expected as first packet




Definition at line 1050 of file
  src/server/client.c.


1051                                                      {
1052   if (!server_ctx || !transport || !client_ip) {
1053     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters to add_webrtc_client");
1054     return NULL;
1055   }
1056 
1057   rwlock_wrlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1058 
1059   // Find empty slot - this is the authoritative check
1060   int slot = -1;
1061   int existing_count = 0;
1062   for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
1063     if (slot == -1 && g_client_manager.clients[i].client_id[0] == '\0') {
1064       slot = i; // Take first available slot
1065     }
1066     // Count only active clients
1067     if (g_client_manager.clients[i].client_id[0] != '\0' && atomic_load_bool(&g_client_manager.clients[i].active)) {
1068       existing_count++;
1069     }
1070   }
1071 
1072   // Check if we've hit the configured max-clients limit (not the array size)
1073   if (existing_count >= GET_OPTION(max_clients)) {
1074     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1075     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED, "Maximum client limit reached (%d/%d active clients)", existing_count,
1076               GET_OPTION(max_clients));
1077     log_error("Maximum client limit reached (%d/%d active clients)", existing_count, GET_OPTION(max_clients));
1078     return NULL;
1079   }
1080 
1081   if (slot == -1) {
1082     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1083     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED, "No available client slots (all %d array slots are in use)", MAX_CLIENTS);
1084     log_error("No available client slots (all %d array slots are in use)", MAX_CLIENTS);
1085     return NULL;
1086   }
1087 
1088   // Update client_count to match actual count before adding new client
1089   g_client_manager.client_count = existing_count;
1090 
1091   // Generate unique client ID for WebRTC client (noun only, without counter or port)
1092   char new_client_id[MAX_CLIENT_ID_LEN];
1093   if (generate_client_id(new_client_id, sizeof(new_client_id)) != 0) {
1094     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1095     log_error("Failed to generate unique client ID for WebRTC client");
1096     return NULL;
1097   }
1098 
1099   // Initialize client
1100   client_info_t *client = &g_client_manager.clients[slot];
1101 
1102   // Free any existing buffers from previous client in this slot
1103   if (client->incoming_video_buffer) {
1104     video_frame_buffer_destroy(client->incoming_video_buffer);
1105     client->incoming_video_buffer = NULL;
1106   }
1107   if (client->outgoing_video_buffer) {
1108     video_frame_buffer_destroy(client->outgoing_video_buffer);
1109     client->outgoing_video_buffer = NULL;
1110   }
1111   if (client->incoming_audio_buffer) {
1112     audio_ring_buffer_destroy(client->incoming_audio_buffer);
1113     client->incoming_audio_buffer = NULL;
1114   }
1115   if (client->send_buffer) {
1116     SAFE_FREE(client->send_buffer);
1117     client->send_buffer = NULL;
1118   }
1119 
1120   memset(client, 0, sizeof(client_info_t));
1121 
1122   // Set up WebRTC-specific fields
1123   client->socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE; // WebRTC has no traditional socket
1124   client->is_tcp_client = false;         // WebRTC client - threads managed directly
1125   client->transport = transport;         // Use provided transport
1126   SAFE_STRNCPY(client->client_id, new_client_id, sizeof(client->client_id) - 1);
1127   SAFE_STRNCPY(client->client_ip, client_ip, sizeof(client->client_ip) - 1);
1128   client->port = 0; // WebRTC doesn't use port numbers
1129   atomic_store_bool(&client->active, true);
1130   client->server_ctx = server_ctx; // Store server context for receive thread cleanup
1131   log_info("Added new WebRTC client %s from %s (transport=%p, slot=%d)", new_client_id, client_ip, transport, slot);
1132   atomic_store_bool(&client->shutting_down, false);
1133   atomic_store_u64(&client->last_rendered_grid_sources, 0); // Render thread updates this
1134   atomic_store_u64(&client->last_sent_grid_sources, 0);     // Send thread updates this
1135   log_debug("WebRTC client slot assigned: client_id=%s assigned to slot %d", client->client_id, slot);
1136   client->connected_at = time(NULL);
1137 
1138   // Initialize crypto context for this client
1139   memset(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx, 0, sizeof(client->crypto_handshake_ctx));
1140   log_debug("[ADD_WEBRTC_CLIENT] Setting crypto_initialized=false (will be set to true after handshake completes)");
1141   // Do NOT set crypto_initialized = true here! The handshake must complete first.
1142   // For WebSocket clients: websocket_client_handler will perform the handshake
1143   // For WebRTC clients: ACDS signaling may have already done crypto (future enhancement)
1144   client->crypto_initialized = false;
1145 
1146   // Initialize pending packet storage (unused for WebRTC, but keep for consistency)
1147   client->pending_packet_type = 0;
1148   client->pending_packet_payload = NULL;
1149   client->pending_packet_length = 0;
1150 
1151   // Use noun-based client name for display_name as well
1152   SAFE_STRNCPY(client->display_name, new_client_id, sizeof(client->display_name) - 1);
1153 
1154   // Create individual video buffer for this client using modern double-buffering
1155   client->incoming_video_buffer = video_frame_buffer_create(new_client_id);
1156   if (!client->incoming_video_buffer) {
1157     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create video buffer for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1158     log_error("Failed to create video buffer for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1159     goto error_cleanup_webrtc;
1160   }
1161 
1162   // Create individual audio buffer for this client
1163   client->incoming_audio_buffer = audio_ring_buffer_create_for_capture();
1164   if (!client->incoming_audio_buffer) {
1165     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create audio buffer for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1166     log_error("Failed to create audio buffer for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1167     goto error_cleanup_webrtc;
1168   }
1169 
1170   // Create packet queues for outgoing data
1171   client->audio_queue = packet_queue_create_with_pools(500, 1000, false);
1172   if (!client->audio_queue) {
1173     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to create audio queue for WebRTC client");
1174     goto error_cleanup_webrtc;
1175   }
1176 
1177   // Create packet queue for async dispatch: received packets waiting to be processed
1178   // Capacity of 100 packets allows buffering of ~10-50 frames (depending on fragmentation)
1179   client->received_packet_queue = packet_queue_create_with_pools(100, 500, false);
1180   if (!client->received_packet_queue) {
1181     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to create received packet queue for client");
1182     goto error_cleanup_webrtc;
1183   }
1184 
1185   // Create outgoing video buffer for ASCII frames (double buffered, no dropping)
1186   client->outgoing_video_buffer = video_frame_buffer_create(new_client_id);
1187   if (!client->outgoing_video_buffer) {
1188     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to create outgoing video buffer for WebRTC client");
1189     goto error_cleanup_webrtc;
1190   }
1191 
1192   // Pre-allocate send buffer to avoid malloc/free in send thread (prevents deadlocks)
1193   client->send_buffer_size = MAX_FRAME_BUFFER_SIZE; // 2MB should handle largest frames
1194   client->send_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC_ALIGNED(client->send_buffer_size, 64, void *);
1195   if (!client->send_buffer) {
1196     log_error("Failed to allocate send buffer for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1197     goto error_cleanup_webrtc;
1198   }
1199 
1200   g_client_manager.client_count = existing_count + 1; // We just added a client
1201   log_debug("Client count updated: now %d clients (added WebRTC client_id=%s to slot %d)",
1202             g_client_manager.client_count, new_client_id, slot);
1203 
1204   // Add client to uthash table for O(1) lookup
1205   HASH_ADD_STR(g_client_manager.clients_by_id, client_id, client);
1206   log_debug("Added WebRTC client %s to uthash table", new_client_id);
1207 
1208   // Release the write lock IMMEDIATELY after adding to hash table
1209   // All subsequent operations (mutex init, mixer registration) don't need global client manager lock
1210   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1211 
1212   // Register this client's audio buffer with the mixer
1213   if (g_audio_mixer && client->incoming_audio_buffer) {
1214     if (mixer_add_source(g_audio_mixer, new_client_id, client->incoming_audio_buffer) < 0) {
1215       log_warn("Failed to add WebRTC client %s to audio mixer", new_client_id);
1216     } else {
1217 #ifdef DEBUG_AUDIO
1218       log_debug("Added WebRTC client %s to audio mixer", new_client_id);
1219 #endif
1220     }
1221   }
1222 
1223   // Initialize mutexes BEFORE creating any threads to prevent race conditions
1224   if (mutex_init(&client->client_state_mutex, "client_state") != 0) {
1225     log_error("Failed to initialize client state mutex for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1226     // Client is already in hash table - use remove_client for proper cleanup
1227     remove_client(server_ctx, new_client_id);
1228     return NULL;
1229   }
1230 
1231   // Initialize send mutex to protect concurrent socket writes
1232   if (mutex_init(&client->send_mutex, "client_send") != 0) {
1233     log_error("Failed to initialize send mutex for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1234     // Client is already in hash table - use remove_client for proper cleanup
1235     remove_client(server_ctx, client->client_id);
1236     return NULL;
1237   }
1238 
1239   // Initialize condition variable for dispatch thread wake-up
1240   if (cond_init(&client->dispatch_queue_cond, "dispatch_queue_cond") != 0) {
1241     log_error("Failed to initialize dispatch queue condition variable for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1242     // Client is already in hash table - use remove_client for proper cleanup
1243     remove_client(server_ctx, client->client_id);
1244     return NULL;
1245   }
1246 
1247   // For WebRTC clients, the capabilities packet will be received by the receive thread
1248   // when it starts. Unlike TCP clients where we handle it synchronously in add_client(),
1249   // WebRTC uses the transport abstraction which handles packet reception automatically.
1250   log_debug("WebRTC client %s initialized - receive thread will process capabilities", new_client_id);
1251 
1252   // Conditionally start threads based on caller preference
1253   // WebSocket handler passes start_threads=false to defer thread startup until after crypto init
1254   // This ensures receive thread doesn't try to process packets before crypto context is ready
1255   if (start_threads) {
1256     log_debug("[ADD_WEBRTC_CLIENT] Starting client threads (receive, render) for client %s...", new_client_id);
1257     if (start_client_threads(server_ctx, client, false) != 0) {
1258       log_error("Failed to start threads for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1259       return NULL;
1260     }
1261     log_debug("Created receive thread for WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1262     log_debug("[ADD_WEBRTC_CLIENT] Receive thread started - thread will now be processing packets", new_client_id);
1263   } else {
1264     log_debug("[ADD_WEBRTC_CLIENT] Deferring thread startup for client %s (caller will start after crypto init)",
1265               new_client_id);
1266   }
1267 
1268   // Send initial server state to the new client
1269   if (send_server_state_to_client(client) != 0) {
1270     log_warn("Failed to send initial server state to WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1271   } else {
1272 #ifdef DEBUG_NETWORK
1273     log_info("Sent initial server state to WebRTC client %s", new_client_id);
1274 #endif
1275   }
1276 
1277   // Send initial server state via ACIP transport
1278   rwlock_rdlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1279   uint32_t connected_count = g_client_manager.client_count;
1280   rwlock_rdunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1281 
1282   server_state_packet_t state;
1283   state.connected_client_count = connected_count;
1284   state.active_client_count = 0; // Will be updated by broadcast thread
1285   memset(state.reserved, 0, sizeof(state.reserved));
1286 
1287   // Convert to network byte order
1288   server_state_packet_t net_state;
1289   net_state.connected_client_count = HOST_TO_NET_U32(state.connected_client_count);
1290   net_state.active_client_count = HOST_TO_NET_U32(state.active_client_count);
1291   memset(net_state.reserved, 0, sizeof(net_state.reserved));
1292 
1293   asciichat_error_t packet_send_result = acip_send_server_state(client->transport, &net_state);
1294   if (packet_send_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1295     log_warn("Failed to send initial server state to WebRTC client %s: %s", new_client_id,
1296              asciichat_error_string(packet_send_result));
1297   } else {
1298     log_debug("Sent initial server state to WebRTC client %s: %u connected clients", new_client_id,
1299               state.connected_client_count);
1300   }
1301 
1302   // Register client with session_host (for discovery mode support)
1303   // WebRTC clients use INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE since they don't have a TCP socket
1304   if (server_ctx->session_host) {
1305     client->session_client_id = session_host_add_client(server_ctx->session_host, INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE, client_ip, 0);
1306     if (client->session_client_id == 0) {
1307       log_warn("Failed to register WebRTC client %s with session_host", new_client_id);
1308     } else {
1309       log_debug("WebRTC client %s registered with session_host as %u", new_client_id, client->session_client_id);
1310     }
1311   }
1312 
1313   // Broadcast server state to ALL clients AFTER the new client is fully set up
1314   // This notifies all clients (including the new one) about the updated grid
1315   broadcast_server_state_to_all_clients();
1316 
1317   return client;
1318 
1319 error_cleanup_webrtc:
1320   // Clean up all partially allocated resources for WebRTC client
1321   // NOTE: This label is reached when allocation or initialization fails BEFORE
1322   // the client is added to the hash table. Don't call remove_client() here.
1323   cleanup_client_all_buffers(client);
1324   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1325   return NULL;
1326 }

void
  broadcast_server_state_to_all_clients (void )


Notify all clients of state changes.


Definition at line 2532 of file
  src/server/client.c.


2532                                                  {
2533   // SNAPSHOT PATTERN: Collect client data while holding lock, then release before network I/O
2534   typedef struct {
2535     socket_t socket;
2536     char client_id[MAX_CLIENT_ID_LEN];
2537     const crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx;
2538   } client_snapshot_t;
2539 
2540   client_snapshot_t client_snapshots[MAX_CLIENTS];
2541   int snapshot_count = 0;
2542   int active_video_count = 0;
2543 
2544   uint64_t lock_start_ns = time_get_ns();
2545   rwlock_rdlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
2546   uint64_t lock_end_ns = time_get_ns();
2547   uint64_t lock_time_ns = time_elapsed_ns(lock_start_ns, lock_end_ns);
2548   if (lock_time_ns > 1 * NS_PER_MS_INT) {
2549     char duration_str[32];
2550     time_pretty((uint64_t)((double)lock_time_ns), -1, duration_str, sizeof(duration_str));
2551     log_warn("broadcast_server_state: rwlock_rdlock took %s", duration_str);
2552   }
2553 
2554   // Count active clients and snapshot client data while holding lock
2555   // Use atomic_load for all atomic fields to prevent data races.
2556   for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
2557     bool is_active = atomic_load_bool(&g_client_manager.clients[i].active);
2558     if (is_active && atomic_load_bool(&g_client_manager.clients[i].is_sending_video)) {
2559       active_video_count++;
2560     }
2561     if (is_active && g_client_manager.clients[i].socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
2562       // Skip clients that haven't completed crypto handshake yet
2563       // Check both crypto_initialized AND crypto context (defense in depth)
2564       if (!GET_OPTION(no_encrypt) && !g_client_manager.clients[i].crypto_initialized) {
2565         log_debug("Skipping server_state broadcast to client %s: crypto handshake not complete",
2566                   g_client_manager.clients[i].client_id);
2567         continue;
2568       }
2569 
2570       // Get crypto context (may be NULL if --no-encrypt)
2571       const crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx =
2572           crypto_handshake_get_context(&g_client_manager.clients[i].crypto_handshake_ctx);
2573       if (!GET_OPTION(no_encrypt) && !crypto_ctx) {
2574         // Skip clients without valid crypto context
2575         log_debug("Skipping server_state broadcast to client %s: no crypto context",
2576                   g_client_manager.clients[i].client_id);
2577         continue;
2578       }
2579 
2580       client_snapshots[snapshot_count].socket = g_client_manager.clients[i].socket;
2581       SAFE_STRNCPY(client_snapshots[snapshot_count].client_id, g_client_manager.clients[i].client_id,
2582                    sizeof(client_snapshots[snapshot_count].client_id) - 1);
2583       client_snapshots[snapshot_count].crypto_ctx = crypto_ctx;
2584       snapshot_count++;
2585     }
2586   }
2587 
2588   // Prepare server state packet while still holding lock (fast operation)
2589   server_state_packet_t state;
2590   state.connected_client_count = g_client_manager.client_count;
2591   state.active_client_count = active_video_count;
2592   memset(state.reserved, 0, sizeof(state.reserved));
2593 
2594   // Convert to network byte order
2595   server_state_packet_t net_state;
2596   net_state.connected_client_count = HOST_TO_NET_U32(state.connected_client_count);
2597   net_state.active_client_count = HOST_TO_NET_U32(state.active_client_count);
2598   memset(net_state.reserved, 0, sizeof(net_state.reserved));
2599 
2600   // Release lock BEFORE sending (snapshot pattern)
2601   // Sending while holding lock blocks all client operations
2602   uint64_t lock_held_final_ns = time_get_ns();
2603   uint64_t lock_held_ns = time_elapsed_ns(lock_start_ns, lock_held_final_ns);
2604   rwlock_rdunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
2605 
2606   // Send to all clients AFTER releasing the lock
2607   // This prevents blocking other threads during network I/O
2608   for (int i = 0; i < snapshot_count; i++) {
2609     log_debug_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT,
2610                     "BROADCAST_DEBUG: Sending SERVER_STATE to client %s (socket %d) with crypto_ctx=%p",
2611                     client_snapshots[i].client_id, client_snapshots[i].socket, (void *)client_snapshots[i].crypto_ctx);
2612 
2613     // Protect socket write with per-client send_mutex.
2614     client_info_t *target = find_client_by_id(client_snapshots[i].client_id);
2615     if (target) {
2616       // IMPORTANT: Verify client_id matches expected value - prevents use-after-free
2617       // if client was removed and replaced with another client in same slot
2618       if (strcmp(target->client_id, client_snapshots[i].client_id) != 0) {
2619         log_warn("Client %s ID mismatch during broadcast (found %s), skipping send", client_snapshots[i].client_id,
2620                  target->client_id);
2621         continue;
2622       }
2623 
2624       mutex_lock(&target->send_mutex);
2625 
2626       // Double-check client_id again after acquiring mutex (stronger protection)
2627       if (strcmp(target->client_id, client_snapshots[i].client_id) != 0) {
2628         mutex_unlock(&target->send_mutex);
2629         log_warn("Client %s was removed during broadcast send (now %s), skipping", client_snapshots[i].client_id,
2630                  target->client_id);
2631         continue;
2632       }
2633 
2634       // Send via ACIP transport
2635       asciichat_error_t result = acip_send_server_state(target->transport, &net_state);
2636       mutex_unlock(&target->send_mutex);
2637 
2638       if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2639         log_error("Failed to send server state to client %s: %s", client_snapshots[i].client_id,
2640                   asciichat_error_string(result));
2641       } else {
2642         log_debug_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT, "Sent server state to client %s: %u connected, %u active",
2643                         client_snapshots[i].client_id, state.connected_client_count, state.active_client_count);
2644       }
2645     } else {
2646       log_warn("Client %s removed before broadcast send could complete", client_snapshots[i].client_id);
2647     }
2648   }
2649 
2650   if (lock_held_ns > 1 * NS_PER_MS_INT) {
2651     char duration_str[32];
2652     time_pretty((uint64_t)((double)lock_held_ns), -1, duration_str, sizeof(duration_str));
2653     log_warn("broadcast_server_state: rwlock held for %s (includes network I/O)", duration_str);
2654   }
2655 }

    server_state_packet_t::connected_client_count,

void
  * client_dispatch_thread (void * arg)


Async dispatch thread for WebRTC clients. Async dispatch thread -
    processes queued received packets.




Decouples receive from dispatch to prevent backpressure on the
    network socket. Received packets are queued by the receive thread, and
    processed asynchronously here. This allows the receive thread to keep
    accepting packets while dispatch processes them.


Definition at line 1633 of file
  src/server/client.c.


1633                                         {
1634   client_info_t *client = (client_info_t *)arg;
1635 
1636   if (!client) {
1637     log_error("Invalid client info in dispatch thread (NULL pointer)");
1638     return NULL;
1639   }
1640 
1641   const char *client_id = client->client_id;
1642   log_info("DISPATCH_THREAD: Started for client %s", client_id);
1643 
1644   uint64_t dispatch_loop_count = 0;
1645 
1646   while (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit) && atomic_load_bool(&client->dispatch_thread_running)) {
1647     dispatch_loop_count++;
1648     // Try to dequeue next packet (non-blocking)
1649     // Use try_dequeue to avoid blocking - allows checking exit flag frequently
1650     uint64_t dequeue_start = time_get_ns();
1651     queued_packet_t *queued_pkt = packet_queue_try_dequeue(client->received_packet_queue);
1652     uint64_t dequeue_end = time_get_ns();
1653 
1654     if (!queued_pkt) {
1655       // Queue was empty, wait for signal from receive thread or timeout
1656       // Use 100ms timeout to handle cases where signal is missed due to race conditions
1657       log_dev_every(5 * US_PER_MS_INT, "🔄 DISPATCH_LOOP[%llu]: Queue empty after %.1fμs, waiting for signal",
1658                     (unsigned long long)dispatch_loop_count, (dequeue_end - dequeue_start) / 1000.0);
1659       mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
1660       cond_timedwait(&client->dispatch_queue_cond, &client->client_state_mutex, 100 * 1000 * 1000); // 100ms timeout
1661       mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
1662       continue;
1663     }
1664 
1665     // Frame received! Log it immediately
1666     char dequeue_elapsed_str[32];
1667     time_pretty(dequeue_end - dequeue_start, -1, dequeue_elapsed_str, sizeof(dequeue_elapsed_str));
1668     log_info("✅ DISPATCH_THREAD[%u] DEQUEUED packet: %zu bytes (dequeue took %s)", client_id, queued_pkt->data_len,
1669              dequeue_elapsed_str);
1670 
1671     // Process the dequeued packet
1672     // The queued packet contains the complete ACIP packet (header + payload) from websocket_recv()
1673     const packet_header_t *header = (const packet_header_t *)queued_pkt->data;
1674     size_t total_len = queued_pkt->data_len;
1675     uint8_t *payload = (uint8_t *)header + sizeof(packet_header_t);
1676     size_t payload_len = 0;
1677 
1678     log_info("🔍 DISPATCH_THREAD[%u]: Processing %zu byte packet (header size=%zu)", client_id, total_len,
1679              sizeof(packet_header_t));
1680 
1681     if (total_len < sizeof(packet_header_t)) {
1682       log_error("🔴 DISPATCH_THREAD[%u]: Packet too small (%zu < %zu), DROPPING", client_id, total_len,
1683                 sizeof(packet_header_t));
1684       packet_queue_free_packet(queued_pkt);
1685       continue;
1686     }
1687 
1688     {
1689       packet_type_t packet_type = (packet_type_t)NET_TO_HOST_U16(header->type);
1690       payload_len = NET_TO_HOST_U32(header->length);
1691 
1692       log_info("🎯 DISPATCH_THREAD[%u]: Packet type=%d, payload_len=%u, total_len=%zu", client_id, packet_type,
1693                payload_len, total_len);
1694 
1695       // Handle PACKET_TYPE_ENCRYPTED from WebSocket clients that encrypt at application layer
1696       // This mirrors the decryption logic in acip_server_receive_and_dispatch()
1697       if (packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_ENCRYPTED && client->transport && client->transport->crypto_ctx) {
1698         log_info("🔐 DISPATCH_THREAD[%u]: Decrypting PACKET_TYPE_ENCRYPTED", client_id);
1699 
1700         uint8_t *ciphertext = payload;
1701         size_t ciphertext_len = payload_len;
1702 
1703         // Decrypt to get inner plaintext packet (header + payload)
1704         size_t plaintext_size = ciphertext_len + 1024;
1705         uint8_t *plaintext = SAFE_MALLOC(plaintext_size, uint8_t *);
1706         if (!plaintext) {
1707           log_error("🔴 DISPATCH_THREAD[%u]: Failed to allocate plaintext buffer for decryption", client_id);
1708           packet_queue_free_packet(queued_pkt);
1709           continue;
1710         }
1711 
1712         size_t plaintext_len;
1713         crypto_result_t crypto_result = crypto_decrypt(client->transport->crypto_ctx, ciphertext, ciphertext_len,
1714                                                        plaintext, plaintext_size, &plaintext_len);
1715 
1716         if (crypto_result != CRYPTO_OK) {
1717           log_error("🔴 DISPATCH_THREAD[%u]: Failed to decrypt packet: %s", client_id,
1718                     crypto_result_to_string(crypto_result));
1719           SAFE_FREE(plaintext);
1720           packet_queue_free_packet(queued_pkt);
1721           continue;
1722         }
1723 
1724         if (plaintext_len < sizeof(packet_header_t)) {
1725           log_error("🔴 DISPATCH_THREAD[%u]: Decrypted packet too small: %zu < %zu", client_id, plaintext_len,
1726                     sizeof(packet_header_t));
1727           SAFE_FREE(plaintext);
1728           packet_queue_free_packet(queued_pkt);
1729           continue;
1730         }
1731 
1732         // Parse the inner (decrypted) header
1733         const packet_header_t *inner_header = (const packet_header_t *)plaintext;
1734         packet_type = (packet_type_t)NET_TO_HOST_U16(inner_header->type);
1735         payload_len = NET_TO_HOST_U32(inner_header->length);
1736         payload = plaintext + sizeof(packet_header_t);
1737 
1738         log_info("🔐 DISPATCH_THREAD[%u]: Decrypted inner packet type=%d, payload_len=%u", client_id, packet_type,
1739                  payload_len);
1740 
1741         // Dispatch the decrypted packet
1742         if (client->transport) {
1743           log_info("🎯 DISPATCH_THREAD[%u]: Calling acip_handle_server_packet(type=%d, payload_len=%u)", client_id,
1744                    packet_type, payload_len);
1745           asciichat_error_t dispatch_result = acip_handle_server_packet(client->transport, packet_type, payload,
1746                                                                         payload_len, client, &g_acip_server_callbacks);
1747 
1748           if (dispatch_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1749             log_error("🔴 DISPATCH_THREAD[%u]: Handler failed for decrypted packet type=%d: %s", client_id, packet_type,
1750                       asciichat_error_string(dispatch_result));
1751           } else {
1752             log_info("✅ DISPATCH_THREAD[%u]: Successfully dispatched decrypted packet type=%d", client_id,
1753                      packet_type);
1754           }
1755         } else {
1756           log_error("🔴 DISPATCH_THREAD[%u]: Cannot dispatch decrypted packet - transport is NULL", client_id);
1757         }
1758 
1759         // Free the decrypted buffer
1760         SAFE_FREE(plaintext);
1761       } else {
1762         // Not encrypted or no crypto context - dispatch as-is
1763         if (client->transport) {
1764           log_info("🎯 DISPATCH_THREAD[%u]: Calling acip_handle_server_packet(type=%d, payload_len=%u)", client_id,
1765                    packet_type, payload_len);
1766           asciichat_error_t dispatch_result = acip_handle_server_packet(client->transport, packet_type, payload,
1767                                                                         payload_len, client, &g_acip_server_callbacks);
1768 
1769           if (dispatch_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1770             log_error("🔴 DISPATCH_THREAD[%u]: Handler failed for packet type=%d: %s", client_id, packet_type,
1771                       asciichat_error_string(dispatch_result));
1772           } else {
1773             log_info("✅ DISPATCH_THREAD[%u]: Successfully dispatched packet type=%d", client_id, packet_type);
1774           }
1775         } else {
1776           log_error("🔴 DISPATCH_THREAD[%u]: Cannot dispatch packet - transport is NULL", client_id);
1777         }
1778       }
1779     }
1780 
1781     // Free the queued packet
1782     packet_queue_free_packet(queued_pkt);
1783   }
1784 
1785   log_info("DISPATCH_THREAD: Exiting for client %s", client_id);
1786   return NULL;
1787 }

void
  * client_receive_thread (void * arg)


Definition at line 1789 of file
  src/server/client.c.


1789                                        {
1790   // Log thread startup
1791   log_debug("RECV_THREAD: Thread function entered, arg=%p", arg);
1792 
1793   client_info_t *client = (client_info_t *)arg;
1794 
1795   // Validate client pointer immediately before any access.
1796   // This prevents crashes if remove_client() has zeroed the client struct
1797   // while the thread was still starting at RtlUserThreadStart.
1798   if (!client) {
1799     log_error("Invalid client info in receive thread (NULL pointer)");
1800     return NULL;
1801   }
1802 
1803   // Save this thread's ID so remove_client() can detect self-joins
1804   client->receive_thread_id = asciichat_thread_self();
1805 
1806   log_debug("RECV_THREAD_DEBUG: Thread started, client=%p, client_id=%s, is_tcp=%d", (void *)client, client->client_id,
1807             client->is_tcp_client);
1808 
1809   if (atomic_load_bool(&client->protocol_disconnect_requested)) {
1810     log_debug("Receive thread for client %s exiting before start (protocol disconnect requested)", client->client_id);
1811     return NULL;
1812   }
1813 
1814   // Check if client_id is empty (client struct has been zeroed by remove_client)
1815   // This must be checked BEFORE accessing any client fields
1816   if (client->client_id[0] == 0) {
1817     log_debug("Receive thread: client_id is empty, client struct may have been zeroed, exiting");
1818     return NULL;
1819   }
1820 
1821   // Additional validation: check socket is valid
1822   // For TCP clients, validate socket. WebRTC clients use DataChannel (no socket)
1823   if (client->is_tcp_client && client->socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1824     log_error("Invalid client socket in receive thread");
1825     return NULL;
1826   }
1827 
1828   // NOTE: Atomic registration disabled
1829   // register_client_info_atomics() and audio_ring_buffer_register_atomics() attempt
1830   // to acquire WRITE lock on named_registry.entries_lock, which conflicts with debug_sync
1831   // thread holding READ lock during printing, causing deadlock.
1832   // These are debug-only features, not critical for functionality.
1833   // The atomics work fine without being registered in the debug system.
1834 
1835   // Enable thread cancellation for clean shutdown
1836   // Thread cancellation not available in platform abstraction
1837   // Threads should exit when g_should_exit is set
1838 
1839   log_debug("Started receive thread for client %s (%s)", client->client_id, client->display_name);
1840 
1841   // Main receive loop - processes packets from transport
1842   // For TCP clients: receives from socket
1843   // For WebRTC clients: receives from transport ringbuffer (via ACDS signaling)
1844 
1845   log_info("RECV_THREAD_LOOP_START: client_id=%s, is_tcp=%d, transport=%p, active=%d", client->client_id,
1846            client->is_tcp_client, (void *)client->transport, atomic_load_bool(&client->active));
1847 
1848   while (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit) && atomic_load_bool(&client->active)) {
1849     // For TCP clients, check socket validity
1850     // For WebRTC clients, continue even if no socket (transport handles everything)
1851     if (client->is_tcp_client && client->socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1852       log_debug("TCP client %s has invalid socket, exiting receive thread", client->client_id);
1853       break;
1854     }
1855 
1856     // Check client_id is still valid before accessing transport.
1857     // This prevents accessing freed memory if remove_client() has zeroed the client struct.
1858     if (client->client_id[0] == 0) {
1859       log_debug("Client client_id reset, exiting receive thread");
1860       break;
1861     }
1862 
1863     // Receive packet (without dispatching) - decouple from dispatch for async processing
1864     // For WebRTC clients with async dispatch, we queue packets instead of processing immediately
1865     // This prevents backpressure on the network socket
1866 
1867     if (client->is_tcp_client) {
1868       // TCP clients: use original synchronous dispatch
1869       asciichat_error_t acip_result =
1870           acip_server_receive_and_dispatch(client->transport, client, &g_acip_server_callbacks);
1871 
1872       // Check if shutdown was requested during the network call
1873       if (atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
1874         log_debug("RECV_EXIT: Server shutdown requested, breaking loop");
1875         break;
1876       }
1877 
1878       // Handle receive errors
1879       if (acip_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1880         asciichat_error_context_t err_ctx;
1881         if (HAS_ERRNO(&err_ctx)) {
1882           log_error("🔴 ACIP error for client %s: code=%u msg=%s", client->client_id, err_ctx.code,
1883                     err_ctx.context_message);
1884           if (err_ctx.code == ERROR_NETWORK) {
1885             log_debug("Client %s disconnected (network error): %s", client->client_id, err_ctx.context_message);
1886             break;
1887           } else if (err_ctx.code == ERROR_CRYPTO) {
1888             log_error_client(
1889                 client, "SECURITY VIOLATION: Unencrypted packet when encryption required - terminating connection");
1890             atomic_store_bool(&g_should_exit, true);
1891             break;
1892           }
1893         }
1894         log_warn("ACIP error for TCP client %s: %s (disconnecting)", client->client_id,
1895                  asciichat_error_string(acip_result));
1896         break;
1897       }
1898     } else {
1899       // WebRTC/WebSocket clients: async dispatch - receive packet and queue for async processing
1900       void *packet_data = NULL;
1901       void *allocated_buffer = NULL;
1902       size_t packet_len = 0;
1903 
1904       // Snapshot transport pointer to prevent use-after-free
1905       // Transport can be destroyed by WebSocket callback while we're receiving
1906       acip_transport_t *transport_snapshot = NULL;
1907       mutex_lock(&client->send_mutex);
1908       if (!client->transport || atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down)) {
1909         mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
1910         log_debug("RECV_THREAD[%s]: Transport destroyed or client shutting down, exiting receive loop",
1911                   client->client_id);
1912         break;
1913       }
1914       transport_snapshot = client->transport;
1915       mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
1916 
1917       log_debug("🔍 RECV_THREAD[%s]: About to call transport->recv() (transport=%p)", client->client_id,
1918                 (void *)transport_snapshot);
1919 
1920       asciichat_error_t recv_result =
1921           transport_snapshot->methods->recv(transport_snapshot, &packet_data, &packet_len, &allocated_buffer);
1922 
1923       // Check error BEFORE logging - logging system may clear thread-local errno
1924       if (recv_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1925         asciichat_error_context_t err_ctx;
1926         if (HAS_ERRNO(&err_ctx)) {
1927           // Check for reassembly timeout (fragments arriving slowly)
1928           // This is NOT a connection failure - safe to retry
1929           if ((err_ctx.code == ERROR_NETWORK) && err_ctx.context_message &&
1930               strstr(err_ctx.context_message, "reassembly timeout")) {
1931             // Fragments are arriving slowly - this is normal, retry without disconnecting
1932             log_dev_every(100000, "Client %s: fragment reassembly timeout, retrying in 10ms", client->client_id);
1933             platform_sleep_ms(10); // Sleep 10ms to allow fragments to arrive
1934             continue;              // Retry without disconnecting
1935           }
1936 
1937           if (err_ctx.code == ERROR_NETWORK) {
1938             log_debug("Client %s disconnected (network error): %s", client->client_id, err_ctx.context_message);
1939             break;
1940           }
1941         }
1942         log_warn("Receive failed for WebRTC client %s: %s (disconnecting)", client->client_id,
1943                  asciichat_error_string(recv_result));
1944         break;
1945       }
1946 
1947       log_info("🔍 RECV_THREAD[%s]: transport->recv() returned result=%d, packet_len=%zu, allocated_buffer=%p",
1948                client->client_id, recv_result, packet_len, allocated_buffer);
1949 
1950       // Validate received packet before queueing
1951       if (packet_len < sizeof(packet_header_t)) {
1952         log_warn("RECV_THREAD[%s]: Received packet too small (%zu < %zu), dropping", client->client_id, packet_len,
1953                  sizeof(packet_header_t));
1954         if (allocated_buffer) {
1955           buffer_pool_free(NULL, allocated_buffer, packet_len);
1956         }
1957         continue;
1958       }
1959 
1960       // Queue the received packet for async dispatch
1961       // This prevents the receive thread from blocking on dispatch
1962       log_info("✅ RECV_THREAD[%s]: Queuing %zu byte packet for async dispatch", client->client_id, packet_len);
1963 
1964       // Extract packet type from the header to preserve it when queueing
1965       const packet_header_t *pkt_header = (const packet_header_t *)allocated_buffer;
1966       packet_type_t pkt_type = (packet_type_t)NET_TO_HOST_U16(pkt_header->type);
1967       uint32_t payload_len = NET_TO_HOST_U32(pkt_header->length);
1968 
1969       log_info("🔍 RECV_THREAD[%s]: Packet header: type=%d, payload_len=%u, total_len=%zu", client->client_id, pkt_type,
1970                payload_len, packet_len);
1971 
1972       // Build a complete packet to queue (header + payload)
1973       // The entire buffer (allocated_buffer) contains the full packet
1974       // copy_data=false because we want to transfer ownership to the queue
1975       uint32_t client_id_hash = fnv1a_hash_string(client->client_id);
1976       int enqueue_result = packet_queue_enqueue(client->received_packet_queue, pkt_type, allocated_buffer, packet_len,
1977                                                 client_id_hash, false);
1978 
1979       if (enqueue_result < 0) {
1980         log_error("🔴 RECV_THREAD[%s]: Failed to queue received packet (queue full?) - DROPPING FRAME",
1981                   client->client_id);
1982         if (allocated_buffer) {
1983           buffer_pool_free(NULL, allocated_buffer, packet_len);
1984         }
1985       } else {
1986         log_info("✅ RECV_THREAD[%s]: Successfully queued packet (type=%d, len=%zu)", client->client_id, pkt_type,
1987                  packet_len);
1988         // Signal dispatch thread to wake up and process the newly queued packet
1989         cond_signal(&client->dispatch_queue_cond);
1990       }
1991     }
1992   }
1993 
1994   // Mark client as inactive and stop all threads
1995   // Must stop render threads when client disconnects.
1996   // OPTIMIZED: Use atomic operations for thread control flags (lock-free)
1997   const char *client_id_snapshot = client->client_id;
1998   log_debug("Setting active=false in receive_thread_fn (client_id=%s, exiting receive loop)", client_id_snapshot);
1999   atomic_store_bool(&client->active, false);
2000   atomic_store_bool(&client->send_thread_running, false);
2001   atomic_store_bool(&client->video_render_thread_running, false);
2002   atomic_store_bool(&client->audio_render_thread_running, false);
2003 
2004   // Call remove_client() to trigger cleanup
2005   // Safe to call from receive thread now: remove_client() detects self-join via thread IDs
2006   // and skips the receive thread join when called from the receive thread itself
2007   log_debug("Receive thread for client %s calling remove_client() for cleanup", client_id_snapshot);
2008   server_context_t *server_ctx = (server_context_t *)client->server_ctx;
2009   if (server_ctx) {
2010     if (remove_client(server_ctx, client_id_snapshot) != 0) {
2011       log_warn("Failed to remove client %s from receive thread cleanup", client_id_snapshot);
2012     }
2013   } else {
2014     log_error("Receive thread for client %s: server_ctx is NULL, cannot call remove_client()", client_id_snapshot);
2015   }
2016 
2017   log_debug("Receive thread for client %s terminated", client_id_snapshot);
2018 
2019   // Clean up thread-local error context before exit
2020   asciichat_errno_destroy();
2021 
2022   return NULL;
2023 }






References acip_server_receive_and_dispatch(),
    client_info::active, asciichat_errno_destroy(),
    ASCIICHAT_OK, asciichat_thread_self(),
    atomic_load_bool(), atomic_store_bool(),

    client_info::client_id, asciichat_error_context_t::code,
    cond_signal(), asciichat_error_context_t::context_message,
    client_info::dispatch_queue_cond, client_info::display_name,
    ERROR_CRYPTO, ERROR_NETWORK, g_should_exit,
    HAS_ERRNO, INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE,
    client_info::is_tcp_client, packet_header_t::length,
    log_debug, log_dev_every, log_error,
    log_error_client, log_info, log_warn,
    acip_transport::methods, mutex_lock, mutex_unlock,
    NET_TO_HOST_U16, NET_TO_HOST_U32,
    packet_queue_enqueue(), platform_sleep_ms(),
    client_info::protocol_disconnect_requested,
    client_info::receive_thread_id,
    client_info::received_packet_queue,
    acip_transport_methods_t::recv, remove_client(),
    client_info::send_mutex, client_info::send_thread_running,
    client_info::server_ctx, client_info::shutting_down,

void
  * client_send_thread_func (void * arg)


Client packet send thread.
