2026                                          {
2027   client_info_t *client = (client_info_t *)arg;
2028 
2029   // Validate client pointer immediately before any access.
2030   // This prevents crashes if remove_client() has zeroed the client struct
2031   // while the thread was still starting at RtlUserThreadStart.
2032   if (!client) {
2033     log_error("Invalid client info in send thread (NULL pointer)");
2034     return NULL;
2035   }
2036 
2037   // Check if client_id is empty (client struct has been zeroed by remove_client)
2038   // This must be checked BEFORE accessing any client fields
2039   if (client->client_id[0] == 0) {
2040     log_debug_every(100 * US_PER_MS_INT,
2041                     "Send thread: client_id is empty, client struct may have been zeroed, exiting");
2042     return NULL;
2043   }
2044 
2045   // Additional validation: check socket OR transport is valid
2046   // For TCP clients: socket is valid
2047   // For WebRTC clients: socket is INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE but transport is valid
2048   mutex_lock(&client->send_mutex);
2049   bool has_socket = (client->socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE);
2050   bool has_transport = (client->transport != NULL);
2051   mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
2052 
2053   log_info("SEND_THREAD_VALIDATION: client_id=%s socket_valid=%d transport_valid=%d transport_ptr=%p",
2054            client->client_id, has_socket, has_transport, (void *)client->transport);
2055 
2056   if (!has_socket && !has_transport) {
2057     log_error("Invalid client connection in send thread (no socket or transport)");
2058     return NULL;
2059   }
2060 
2061   log_info("Started send thread for client %s (%s)", client->client_id, client->display_name);
2062 
2063   // Mark thread as running
2064   atomic_store_bool(&client->send_thread_running, true);
2065 
2066   log_info("SEND_THREAD_LOOP_START: client_id=%s active=%d shutting_down=%d running=%d", client->client_id,
2067            atomic_load_bool(&client->active), atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down),
2068            atomic_load_bool(&client->send_thread_running));
2069 
2070   // Track timing for video frame sends
2071   uint64_t last_video_send_time = 0;
2072   const uint64_t video_send_interval_us = 16666; // 60fps = ~16.67ms
2073 
2074   // High-frequency audio loop - separate from video frame loop
2075   // to ensure audio packets are sent immediately, not rate-limited by video
2076 #define MAX_AUDIO_BATCH 8
2077   int loop_iteration_count = 0;
2078   while (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit) && !atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down) &&
2079          atomic_load_bool(&client->active) && atomic_load_bool(&client->send_thread_running)) {
2080     loop_iteration_count++;
2081     bool sent_something = false;
2082     uint64_t loop_start_ns = time_get_ns();
2083     log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT, "[SEND_LOOP_%d] START: client=%s", loop_iteration_count, client->client_id);
2084 
2085     // PRIORITY: Drain all queued audio packets before video
2086     // Audio must not be rate-limited by video frame sending (16.67ms)
2087     queued_packet_t *audio_packets[MAX_AUDIO_BATCH];
2088     int audio_packet_count = 0;
2089 
2090     if (client->audio_queue) {
2091       // Try to dequeue multiple audio packets
2092       for (int i = 0; i < MAX_AUDIO_BATCH; i++) {
2093         audio_packets[i] = packet_queue_try_dequeue(client->audio_queue);
2094         if (audio_packets[i]) {
2095           audio_packet_count++;
2096         } else {
2097           break; // No more packets available
2098         }
2099       }
2100       if (audio_packet_count > 0) {
2101         log_dev_every(4500 * US_PER_MS_INT, "SEND_AUDIO: client=%s dequeued=%d packets", client->client_id,
2102                       audio_packet_count);
2103       }
2104     } else {
2105       log_warn("Send thread: audio_queue is NULL for client %s", client->client_id);
2106     }
2107 
2108     // Send batched audio if we have packets
2109     if (audio_packet_count > 0) {
2110       // Protect crypto field access with mutex
2111       mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
2112       bool crypto_ready = !GET_OPTION(no_encrypt) && client->crypto_initialized &&
2113                           crypto_handshake_is_ready(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx);
2114       mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
2115 
2116       // Audio packets cannot be sent until crypto handshake completes (protocol requirement)
2117       if (!crypto_ready) {
2118         log_dev_every(1000 * US_PER_MS_INT, "SKIP_AUDIO: client=%s handshake not ready, dropping %d audio packets",
2119                       client->client_id, audio_packet_count);
2120         // Drop audio packets until handshake completes
2121         for (int i = 0; i < audio_packet_count; i++) {
2122           packet_queue_free_packet(audio_packets[i]);
2123         }
2124         audio_packet_count = 0;
2125       }
2126 
2127       asciichat_error_t result = ASCIICHAT_OK;
2128 
2129       // Only send audio if packets survived crypto check (count > 0 after potential drop)
2130       if (audio_packet_count > 0) {
2131         if (audio_packet_count == 1) {
2132           // Single packet - send directly for low latency using ACIP transport
2133           packet_type_t pkt_type = (packet_type_t)NET_TO_HOST_U16(audio_packets[0]->header.type);
2134 
2135           // Get transport reference while holding mutex briefly (prevents deadlock on TCP buffer full)
2136           mutex_lock(&client->send_mutex);
2137           if (atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down) || !client->transport) {
2138             mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
2139             log_warn("BREAK_AUDIO_SINGLE: client_id=%s shutting_down=%d transport=%p", client->client_id,
2140                      atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down), (void *)client->transport);
2141             break; // Client is shutting down, exit thread
2142           }
2143           acip_transport_t *transport = client->transport;
2144           mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
2145 
2146           // Network I/O happens OUTSIDE the mutex
2147           uint32_t client_id_hash = fnv1a_hash_string(client->client_id);
2148           result = packet_send_via_transport(transport, pkt_type, audio_packets[0]->data, audio_packets[0]->data_len,
2149                                              client_id_hash);
2150           if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2151             log_error("AUDIO SEND FAIL: client=%s, len=%zu, result=%d", client->client_id, audio_packets[0]->data_len,
2152                       result);
2153           }
2154         } else {
2155           // Multiple packets - batch them together and send via transport (works for all client types)
2156           packet_type_t first_pkt_type = (packet_type_t)NET_TO_HOST_U16(audio_packets[0]->header.type);
2157 
2158           // Get transport reference
2159           mutex_lock(&client->send_mutex);
2160           if (atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down) || !client->transport) {
2161             mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
2162             log_warn("BREAK_AUDIO_BATCH: client_id=%s shutting_down=%d transport=%p", client->client_id,
2163                      atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down), (void *)client->transport);
2164             result = ERROR_NETWORK;
2165           } else {
2166             acip_transport_t *transport = client->transport;
2167             mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
2168 
2169             if (first_pkt_type == PACKET_TYPE_AUDIO_OPUS_BATCH) {
2170               // Opus packets - batch and send via transport
2171               size_t total_opus_size = 0;
2172               for (int i = 0; i < audio_packet_count; i++) {
2173                 total_opus_size += audio_packets[i]->data_len;
2174               }
2175 
2176               uint8_t *batched_opus = SAFE_MALLOC(total_opus_size, uint8_t *);
2177               uint16_t *frame_sizes = SAFE_MALLOC((size_t)audio_packet_count * sizeof(uint16_t), uint16_t *);
2178 
2179               if (batched_opus && frame_sizes) {
2180                 size_t offset = 0;
2181                 for (int i = 0; i < audio_packet_count; i++) {
2182                   frame_sizes[i] = (uint16_t)audio_packets[i]->data_len;
2183                   memcpy(batched_opus + offset, audio_packets[i]->data, audio_packets[i]->data_len);
2184                   offset += audio_packets[i]->data_len;
2185                 }
2186                 result = acip_send_audio_opus_batch(transport, batched_opus, total_opus_size, frame_sizes,
2187                                                     (uint32_t)audio_packet_count, AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE, 20);
2188                 if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2189                   log_error("AUDIO SEND FAIL (opus batch): client=%u, frames=%d, total_size=%zu, result=%d",
2190                             client->client_id, audio_packet_count, total_opus_size, result);
2191                 }
2192               } else {
2193                 log_error("Failed to allocate buffer for Opus batch");
2194                 result = ERROR_MEMORY;
2195               }
2196               SAFE_FREE(batched_opus);
2197               SAFE_FREE(frame_sizes);
2198             } else {
2199               // Raw float audio - batch and send via transport
2200               size_t total_samples = 0;
2201               for (int i = 0; i < audio_packet_count; i++) {
2202                 total_samples += audio_packets[i]->data_len / sizeof(float);
2203               }
2204 
2205               float *batched_audio = SAFE_MALLOC(total_samples * sizeof(float), float *);
2206               if (batched_audio) {
2207                 size_t offset = 0;
2208                 for (int i = 0; i < audio_packet_count; i++) {
2209                   size_t packet_samples = audio_packets[i]->data_len / sizeof(float);
2210                   memcpy(batched_audio + offset, audio_packets[i]->data, audio_packets[i]->data_len);
2211                   offset += packet_samples;
2212                 }
2213                 result = acip_send_audio_batch(transport, batched_audio, (uint32_t)total_samples, AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE);
2214                 if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2215                   log_error("AUDIO SEND FAIL (raw batch): client=%u, packets=%d, samples=%zu, result=%d",
2216                             client->client_id, audio_packet_count, total_samples, result);
2217                 }
2218               } else {
2219                 log_error("Failed to allocate buffer for audio batch");
2220                 result = ERROR_MEMORY;
2221               }
2222               SAFE_FREE(batched_audio);
2223             }
2224           }
2225         }
2226       } // End of if (audio_packet_count > 0)
2227 
2228       // Free all audio packets
2229       for (int i = 0; i < audio_packet_count; i++) {
2230         packet_queue_free_packet(audio_packets[i]);
2231       }
2232 
2233       if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2234         if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2235           log_error("Failed to send audio to client %s: %s", client->client_id, asciichat_error_string(result));
2236         }
2237         // Network errors corrupt the TCP stream - must disconnect immediately
2238         if (result == ERROR_NETWORK) {
2239           log_error("CLOSING_CLIENT_ON_NETWORK_ERROR: client_id=%s due to audio send failure (corrupted stream)",
2240                     client->client_id);
2241           break; // Exit send loop to trigger cleanup
2242         }
2243         log_warn("SKIP_AUDIO_ERROR: client_id=%s result=%d (continuing to send video)", client->client_id, result);
2244         // Continue sending video for non-network errors (audio is optional for browser clients)
2245       }
2246 
2247       sent_something = true;
2248       uint64_t audio_done_ns = time_get_ns();
2249       char audio_elapsed_str[32];
2250       time_pretty(audio_done_ns - loop_start_ns, -1, audio_elapsed_str, sizeof(audio_elapsed_str));
2251       log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT, "[SEND_LOOP_%d] AUDIO_SENT: took %s", loop_iteration_count,
2252                      audio_elapsed_str);
2253 
2254       // Small sleep to let more audio packets queue (helps batching efficiency)
2255       if (audio_packet_count > 0) {
2256         platform_sleep_us(100); // 0.1ms - minimal delay
2257       }
2258     } else {
2259       // No audio packets - brief sleep to avoid busy-looping, then check for other tasks
2260       log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT, "[SEND_LOOP_%d] NO_AUDIO: sleeping 1ms", loop_iteration_count);
2261       platform_sleep_us(1 * US_PER_MS_INT); // 1ms - enough for audio render thread to queue more packets
2262 
2263       // Check if session rekeying should be triggered
2264       mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
2265       bool should_rekey = !GET_OPTION(no_encrypt) && client->crypto_initialized &&
2266                           crypto_handshake_is_ready(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx) &&
2267                           crypto_handshake_should_rekey(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx);
2268       mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
2269 
2270       if (should_rekey) {
2271         log_debug("Rekey threshold reached for client %s, initiating session rekey", client->client_id);
2272         mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
2273         // Get socket reference briefly to avoid deadlock on TCP buffer full
2274         mutex_lock(&client->send_mutex);
2275         if (atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down) || client->socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
2276           mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
2277           mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
2278           log_warn("BREAK_REKEY: client_id=%s shutting_down=%d socket=%d", client->client_id,
2279                    atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down), (int)client->socket);
2280           break; // Client is shutting down, exit thread
2281         }
2282         socket_t rekey_socket = client->socket;
2283         mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
2284 
2285         // Network I/O happens OUTSIDE the send_mutex (client_state_mutex still held for crypto state)
2286         asciichat_error_t result = crypto_handshake_rekey_request(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx, rekey_socket);
2287         mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
2288 
2289         if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2290           log_error("Failed to send REKEY_REQUEST to client %s: %d", client->client_id, result);
2291         } else {
2292           log_debug("Sent REKEY_REQUEST to client %s", client->client_id);
2293           // Notify client that session rekeying has been initiated (old keys still active)
2294           log_info_client(client, "Session rekey initiated - rotating encryption keys");
2295         }
2296       }
2297     }
2298 
2299     // Always consume frames from the buffer to prevent accumulation
2300     // Rate-limit the actual sending, but always mark frames as consumed
2301     uint64_t video_check_ns = time_get_ns();
2302     if (!client->outgoing_video_buffer) {
2303       // Buffer has been destroyed (client is shutting down).
2304       // Exit cleanly instead of looping forever trying to access freed memory.
2305       log_warn("⚠️  Send thread exiting: outgoing_video_buffer is NULL for client %s (client shutting down?)",
2306                client->client_id);
2307       break;
2308     }
2309 
2310     // Get latest frame from double buffer (lock-free operation)
2311     // This marks the frame as consumed even if we don't send it yet
2312     const video_frame_t *frame = video_frame_get_latest(client->outgoing_video_buffer);
2313     uint64_t frame_get_ns = time_get_ns();
2314     char frame_get_elapsed_str[32];
2315     time_pretty(frame_get_ns - video_check_ns, -1, frame_get_elapsed_str, sizeof(frame_get_elapsed_str));
2316     log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT, "[SEND_LOOP_%d] VIDEO_GET_FRAME: took %s, frame=%p", loop_iteration_count,
2317                    frame_get_elapsed_str, (void *)frame);
2318     log_dev_every(4500 * US_PER_MS_INT, "Send thread: video_frame_get_latest returned %p for client %s", (void *)frame,
2319                   client->client_id);
2320 
2321     // Check if get_latest failed (buffer might have been destroyed)
2322     if (!frame) {
2323       log_warn("⚠️  Send thread exiting: video_frame_get_latest returned NULL for client %s (buffer destroyed?)",
2324                client->client_id);
2325       break; // Exit thread if buffer is invalid
2326     }
2327 
2328     // Check if it's time to send a video frame (60fps rate limiting)
2329     // Only rate-limit the SEND operation, not frame consumption
2330     uint64_t current_time_ns = time_get_ns();
2331     uint64_t current_time_us = time_ns_to_us(current_time_ns);
2332     uint64_t time_since_last_send_us = current_time_us - last_video_send_time;
2333     log_dev_every(4500 * US_PER_MS_INT,
2334                   "Send thread timing check: time_since_last=%llu us, interval=%llu us, should_send=%d",
2335                   (unsigned long long)time_since_last_send_us, (unsigned long long)video_send_interval_us,
2336                   (time_since_last_send_us >= video_send_interval_us));
2337 
2338     if (current_time_us - last_video_send_time >= video_send_interval_us) {
2339       log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT, "✓ SEND_TIME_READY: client_id=%s time_since=%llu interval=%llu",
2340                      client->client_id, (unsigned long long)time_since_last_send_us,
2341                      (unsigned long long)video_send_interval_us);
2342       uint64_t frame_start_ns = time_get_ns();
2343 
2344       // GRID LAYOUT CHANGE: Check if render thread has buffered a frame with different source count
2345       // If so, send CLEAR_CONSOLE before sending the new frame
2346       int rendered_sources = atomic_load_bool(&client->last_rendered_grid_sources);
2347       int sent_sources = atomic_load_bool(&client->last_sent_grid_sources);
2348 
2349       if (rendered_sources != sent_sources && rendered_sources > 0) {
2350         // Grid layout changed! Send CLEAR_CONSOLE before next frame using ACIP transport
2351         // Get transport reference briefly to avoid deadlock on TCP buffer full
2352         mutex_lock(&client->send_mutex);
2353         if (atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down) || !client->transport) {
2354           mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
2355           log_warn("BREAK_CLEAR_CONSOLE: client_id=%s shutting_down=%d transport=%p", client->client_id,
2356                    atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down), (void *)client->transport);
2357           break; // Client is shutting down, exit thread
2358         }
2359         acip_transport_t *clear_transport = client->transport;
2360         mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
2361 
2362         // Network I/O happens OUTSIDE the mutex
2363         acip_send_clear_console(clear_transport);
2364         log_debug_every(LOG_RATE_FAST, "Client %s: Sent CLEAR_CONSOLE (grid changed %d → %d sources)",
2365                         client->client_id, sent_sources, rendered_sources);
2366         atomic_store_u64(&client->last_sent_grid_sources, rendered_sources);
2367         sent_something = true;
2368       }
2369 
2370       log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT,
2371                      "🎯 FRAME_VALIDATION: client_id=%s frame=%p, frame->data=%p, frame->size=%zu", client->client_id,
2372                      (void *)frame, (void *)frame->data, frame->size);
2373 
2374       if (!frame->data) {
2375         log_info("❌ SKIP_NO_DATA: client_id=%s frame=%p data=%p", client->client_id, (void *)frame,
2376                  (void *)frame->data);
2377         continue;
2378       }
2379       log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT, "✅ FRAME_DATA_OK: client_id=%s data=%p", client->client_id,
2380                      (void *)frame->data);
2381 
2382       if (frame->data && frame->size == 0) {
2383         log_info("❌ SKIP_ZERO_SIZE: client_id=%s size=%zu", client->client_id, frame->size);
2384         platform_sleep_us(1 * US_PER_MS_INT); // 1ms sleep
2385         continue;
2386       }
2387       log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT, "✅ FRAME_SIZE_OK: client_id=%s size=%zu", client->client_id, frame->size);
2388 
2389       // Snapshot frame metadata (safe with double-buffer system)
2390       const char *frame_data = (const char *)frame->data; // Pointer snapshot - data is stable in front buffer
2391       size_t frame_size = frame->size;                    // Size snapshot - prevent race condition with render thread
2392       uint32_t width = client->width;
2393       uint32_t height = client->height;
2394       uint64_t step1_ns = time_get_ns();
2395       uint64_t step2_ns = time_get_ns();
2396       uint64_t step3_ns = time_get_ns();
2397       uint64_t step4_ns = time_get_ns();
2398 
2399       // Check if crypto handshake is complete before sending (prevents sending to unauthenticated clients)
2400       mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
2401       bool crypto_ready = GET_OPTION(no_encrypt) ||
2402                           (client->crypto_initialized && crypto_handshake_is_ready(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx));
2403       mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
2404 
2405       log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT,
2406                      "🔐 CRYPTO_CHECK: client_id=%s crypto_ready=%d crypto_initialized=%d no_encrypt=%d",
2407                      client->client_id, crypto_ready, client->crypto_initialized, GET_OPTION(no_encrypt));
2408 
2409       if (!crypto_ready) {
2410         log_info("⚠️  SKIP_SEND_CRYPTO: client_id=%s crypto_initialized=%d no_encrypt=%d", client->client_id,
2411                  client->crypto_initialized, GET_OPTION(no_encrypt));
2412         continue; // Skip this frame, will try again on next loop iteration
2413       }
2414       log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT, "✅ CRYPTO_READY: client_id=%s about to send frame", client->client_id);
2415 
2416       // Get transport reference briefly to avoid deadlock on TCP buffer full
2417       // ACIP transport handles header building, CRC32, encryption internally
2418       log_dev_every(4500 * US_PER_MS_INT,
2419                     "Send thread: About to send frame to client %s (width=%u, height=%u, size=%zu, data=%p)",
2420                     client->client_id, width, height, frame_size, (void *)frame_data);
2421 
2422       // Log first 32 bytes of frame data to verify we can access it
2423       if (frame_data && frame_size > 0) {
2424         log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT,
2425                        "FRAME_DATA_HEX: client=%u first_bytes=[%02x %02x %02x %02x %02x %02x %02x %02x %02x %02x %02x "
2426                        "%02x %02x %02x %02x %02x]",
2427                        client->client_id, ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[0], ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[1],
2428                        ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[2], ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[3], ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[4],
2429                        ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[5], ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[6], ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[7],
2430                        ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[8], ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[9], ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[10],
2431                        ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[11], ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[12], ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[13],
2432                        ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[14], ((uint8_t *)frame_data)[15]);
2433       }
2434 
2435       mutex_lock(&client->send_mutex);
2436       if (atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down) || !client->transport) {
2437         mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
2438         log_warn("BREAK_FRAME_SEND: client_id=%s shutting_down=%d transport=%p loop_iter=%d", client->client_id,
2439                  atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down), (void *)client->transport, loop_iteration_count);
2440         break; // Client is shutting down, exit thread
2441       }
2442       acip_transport_t *frame_transport = client->transport;
2443       mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
2444 
2445       // Network I/O happens OUTSIDE the mutex
2446       log_dev_every(4500 * US_PER_MS_INT, "SEND_ASCII_FRAME: client_id=%s size=%zu width=%u height=%u",
2447                     client->client_id, frame_size, width, height);
2448       uint64_t send_start_ns = time_get_ns();
2449       log_dev("[SEND_LOOP_%d] FRAME_SEND_START: size=%zu", loop_iteration_count, frame_size);
2450       asciichat_error_t send_result =
2451           acip_send_ascii_frame(frame_transport, frame_data, frame_size, width, height, client->client_id);
2452       uint64_t send_end_ns = time_get_ns();
2453       char send_elapsed_str[32];
2454       time_pretty(send_end_ns - send_start_ns, -1, send_elapsed_str, sizeof(send_elapsed_str));
2455       log_dev("[SEND_LOOP_%d] FRAME_SEND_END: took %s, result=%d", loop_iteration_count, send_elapsed_str, send_result);
2456       uint64_t step5_ns = time_get_ns();
2457 
2458       if (send_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2459         if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2460           SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send video frame to client %s: %s", client->client_id,
2461                     asciichat_error_string(send_result));
2462         }
2463         log_error("SEND_FRAME_FAILED: client_id=%s result=%d message=%s loop_iter=%d", client->client_id, send_result,
2464                   asciichat_error_string(send_result), loop_iteration_count);
2465         log_warn("BREAK_SEND_ERROR: client_id=%s (frame send failed)", client->client_id);
2466         break;
2467       }
2468 
2469       log_dev_every(4500 * US_PER_MS_INT, "SEND_FRAME_SUCCESS: client_id=%s size=%zu", client->client_id, frame_size);
2470 
2471       // Increment frame counter and log
2472       unsigned long frame_count = atomic_fetch_add_u64(&client->frames_sent_count, 1) + 1;
2473       log_info("🎬 FRAME_SENT: client_id=%s frame_num=%lu size=%zu", client->client_id, frame_count, frame_size);
2474 
2475       sent_something = true;
2476       last_video_send_time = current_time_us;
2477 
2478       uint64_t frame_end_ns = time_get_ns();
2479       uint64_t frame_time_us = time_ns_to_us(time_elapsed_ns(frame_start_ns, frame_end_ns));
2480       if (frame_time_us > 15 * US_PER_MS_INT) { // Log if sending a frame takes > 15ms (encryption adds ~5-6ms)
2481         uint64_t step1_us = time_ns_to_us(time_elapsed_ns(frame_start_ns, step1_ns));
2482         uint64_t step2_us = time_ns_to_us(time_elapsed_ns(step1_ns, step2_ns));
2483         uint64_t step3_us = time_ns_to_us(time_elapsed_ns(step2_ns, step3_ns));
2484         uint64_t step4_us = time_ns_to_us(time_elapsed_ns(step3_ns, step4_ns));
2485         uint64_t step5_us = time_ns_to_us(time_elapsed_ns(step4_ns, step5_ns));
2486         log_warn_every(
2487             LOG_RATE_DEFAULT,
2488             "SEND_THREAD: Frame send took %.2fms for client %s | Snapshot: %.2fms | Memcpy: %.2fms | CRC32: %.2fms | "
2489             "Header: %.2fms | send_packet_secure: %.2fms",
2490             frame_time_us / 1000.0, client->client_id, step1_us / 1000.0, step2_us / 1000.0, step3_us / 1000.0,
2491             step4_us / 1000.0, step5_us / 1000.0);
2492       }
2493     }
2494 
2495     // If we didn't send anything, sleep briefly to prevent busy waiting
2496     if (!sent_something) {
2497       log_info_every(5000 * US_PER_MS_INT, "[SEND_LOOP_%d] IDLE_SLEEP: nothing sent", loop_iteration_count);
2498       platform_sleep_us(1 * US_PER_MS_INT); // 1ms sleep
2499     }
2500     uint64_t loop_end_ns = time_get_ns();
2501     uint64_t loop_elapsed_ns = loop_end_ns - loop_start_ns;
2502     if (loop_elapsed_ns > 10 * NS_PER_MS) {
2503       char loop_elapsed_str[32];
2504       time_pretty(loop_elapsed_ns, -1, loop_elapsed_str, sizeof(loop_elapsed_str));
2505       log_warn("[SEND_LOOP_%d] SLOW_ITERATION: took %s (client=%u)", loop_iteration_count, loop_elapsed_str,
2506                client->client_id);
2507     }
2508   }
2509 
2510   // Log why the send thread exited
2511   log_warn("Send thread exit conditions - client_id=%s g_should_exit=%d shutting_down=%d active=%d "
2512            "send_thread_running=%d",
2513            client->client_id, atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit), atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down),
2514            atomic_load_bool(&client->active), atomic_load_bool(&client->send_thread_running));
2515 
2516   // Mark thread as stopped
2517   atomic_store_bool(&client->send_thread_running, false);
2518   log_debug("Send thread for client %s terminated", client->client_id);
2519 
2520   // Clean up thread-local error context before exit
2521   asciichat_errno_destroy();
2522 
2523   return NULL;
2524 }






References acip_send_ascii_frame(),
    acip_send_audio_batch(), acip_send_audio_opus_batch(),
    acip_send_clear_console(), client_info::active,
    asciichat_errno_destroy(), ASCIICHAT_OK,
    atomic_fetch_add_u64(), atomic_load_bool(),
    atomic_store_bool(), atomic_store_u64(),
    client_info::audio_queue, AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE,
    client_info::client_id, client_info::client_state_mutex,

    crypto_handshake_is_ready(), crypto_handshake_rekey_request(),

    client_info::display_name, ERROR_MEMORY, ERROR_NETWORK,
    frame, client_info::frames_sent_count, g_should_exit,
    GET_OPTION, client_info::height, INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE,

client_info_t * find_client_by_id (const char *
  client_id)


Fast O(1) client lookup by ID using hash table. This is the
    primary method for locating clients throughout the server. It uses a hash
    table for constant-time lookups regardless of client count, making it
    suitable for high-performance operations like rendering and stats.




PERFORMANCE CHARACTERISTICS:


    
  
  • Time Complexity: O(1) average case, O(n) worst case (hash collision)
    • 
  
  • Space Complexity: O(1)
    • 
  
  • Thread Safety: Hash table is internally thread-safe for lookups
    • 



USAGE PATTERNS:


    
  
  • Called by render threads to find target clients for frame generation
    • 
  
  • Used by protocol handlers to locate clients for packet processing
    • 
  
  • Stats collection for per-client performance monitoring
    • 



Parameters


client_id Unique identifier for the client (0 is
  invalid)


Returns


Pointer to client_info_t if found, NULL if not
  found or invalid ID


Note


Does not require external locking - hash table provides
  thread safety



Returns direct pointer to client struct - caller should use
    snapshot pattern




Definition at line 388 of file
  src/server/client.c.


388                                                         {
389   if (!client_id || client_id[0] == '\0') {
390     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid client ID");
391     return NULL;
392   }
393 
394   // Protect uthash lookup with read lock to prevent concurrent access issues
395   rwlock_rdlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
396 
397   client_info_t *result = NULL;
398   HASH_FIND_STR(g_client_manager.clients_by_id, client_id, result);
399 
400   rwlock_rdunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
401 
402   if (!result) {
403     log_warn("Client not found for ID %s", client_id);
404   }
405 
406   return result;
407 }






References ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, g_client_manager,
    g_client_manager_rwlock, log_warn, rwlock_rdlock,
    rwlock_rdunlock, and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by broadcast_server_state_to_all_clients(),
    crypto_server_cleanup_client(),
    crypto_server_decrypt_packet(),
    crypto_server_encrypt_packet(), crypto_server_get_context(),
    crypto_server_is_ready(), and
  start_webrtc_client_threads().






client_info_t * find_client_by_socket (socket_t
  socket)


Find client by socket descriptor using linear search. This
    function provides socket-based client lookup, primarily used during
    connection establishment before client IDs are assigned. Less efficient than
    find_client_by_id() but necessary for socket-based operations.




PERFORMANCE CHARACTERISTICS:


    
  
  • Time Complexity: O(n) where n = number of active clients
    • 
  
  • Space Complexity: O(1)
    • 
  
  • Thread Safety: Internally acquires read lock on
    g_client_manager_rwlock
    • 



USAGE PATTERNS:


    
  
  • Connection establishment during add_client() processing
    • 
  
  • Socket error handling and cleanup operations
    • 
  
  • Debugging and diagnostic functions
    • 



Parameters


socket Platform-abstracted socket descriptor to
  search for


Returns


Pointer to client_info_t if found, NULL if not
  found


Note


Only searches active clients (avoids returning stale
  entries)



Caller should use snapshot pattern when accessing returned client
    data




Definition at line 432 of file
  src/server/client.c.


432                                                       {
433   rwlock_rdlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
434 
435   for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
436     if (g_client_manager.clients[i].socket == socket && atomic_load_bool(&g_client_manager.clients[i].active)) {
437       client_info_t *client = &g_client_manager.clients[i];
438       rwlock_rdunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
439       return client;
440     }
441   }
442 
443   rwlock_rdunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
444   return NULL;
445 }

Parameters


client Client info structure


type Packet type


data Packet data


len Packet length

3752   if (type == 5000) { // CLIENT_CAPABILITIES
3753     log_debug("CLIENT: client_id=%s, data=%p, len=%zu", client->client_id, data, len);
3754   }
3755 
3756   // Rate limiting: Check and record packet-specific rate limits
3757   if (g_rate_limiter) {
3758     if (!check_and_record_packet_rate_limit(g_rate_limiter, client->client_ip, client->socket, type)) {
3759       // Rate limit exceeded - error response already sent by utility function
3760       return;
3761     }
3762   }
3763 
3764   // O(1) dispatch via hash table lookup
3765   int idx = client_dispatch_hash_lookup(g_client_dispatch_hash, type);
3766   if (type == 5000 || type == 3001) {
3767     log_error("DISPATCH_LOOKUP: type=%d, idx=%d (len=%zu)", type, idx, len);
3768   }
3769   if (idx < 0) {
3770     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "Unknown packet type: %d (len=%zu)", type, len);
3771     return;
3772   }
3773 
3774   if (type == 5000 || type == 3001) {
3775     log_error("DISPATCH_HANDLER: type=%d, calling handler[%d]...", type, idx);
3776   }
3777   g_client_dispatch_handlers[idx](client, data, len);
3778   if (type == 5000 || type == 3001) {
3779     log_error("DISPATCH_HANDLER: type=%d, handler returned", type);
3780   }
3781 }

int
  process_encrypted_packet (client_info_t * client, packet_type_t
  * type, void ** data, size_t * len, uint32_t * sender_id)


Process an encrypted packet from a client




Parameters


client Client info structure


type Pointer to packet type (will be updated with decrypted type)


data Pointer to packet data (will be updated with decrypted data)


len Pointer to packet length (will be updated with decrypted length)


sender_id Pointer to sender ID (will be updated with decrypted sender
  ID)


Returns


0 on success, -1 on error

  src/server/client.c.


2810                                                   {
2811   if (!crypto_server_is_ready(client->client_id)) {
2812     log_error("Received encrypted packet but crypto not ready for client %s", client->client_id);
2813     buffer_pool_free(NULL, *data, *len);
2814     *data = NULL;
2815     return -1;
2816   }
2817 
2818   // Store original allocation size before it gets modified
2819   size_t original_alloc_size = *len;
2820   void *decrypted_data = buffer_pool_alloc(NULL, original_alloc_size);
2821   size_t decrypted_len;
2822   int decrypt_result = crypto_server_decrypt_packet(client->client_id, (const uint8_t *)*data, *len,
2823                                                     (uint8_t *)decrypted_data, original_alloc_size, &decrypted_len);
2824 
2825   if (decrypt_result != 0) {
2826     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Failed to process encrypted packet from client %s (result=%d)", client->client_id,
2827               decrypt_result);
2828     buffer_pool_free(NULL, *data, original_alloc_size);
2829     buffer_pool_free(NULL, decrypted_data, original_alloc_size);
2830     *data = NULL;
2831     return -1;
2832   }
2833 
2834   // Replace encrypted data with decrypted data
2835   // Use original allocation size for freeing the encrypted buffer
2836   buffer_pool_free(NULL, *data, original_alloc_size);
2837 
2838   *data = decrypted_data;
2839   *len = decrypted_len;
2840 
2841   // Now process the decrypted packet by parsing its header
2842   if (*len < sizeof(packet_header_t)) {
2843     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Decrypted packet too small for header from client %s", client->client_id);
2844     buffer_pool_free(NULL, *data, *len);
2845     *data = NULL;
2846     return -1;
2847   }
2848 
2849   packet_header_t *header = (packet_header_t *)*data;
2850   *type = (packet_type_t)NET_TO_HOST_U16(header->type);
2851   *sender_id = NET_TO_HOST_U32(header->client_id);
2852 
2853   // Adjust data pointer to skip header
2854   *data = (uint8_t *)*data + sizeof(packet_header_t);
2855   *len -= sizeof(packet_header_t);
2856 
2857   return 0;
2858 }

int
  remove_client (server_context_t * server_ctx, const char *
  client_id)


Definition at line 1328 of file
  src/server/client.c.

1330     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Cannot remove client %s: NULL server_ctx", client_id);
1331     return -1;
1332   }
1333 
1334   // Phase 1: Mark client inactive and prepare for cleanup while holding write lock
1335   client_info_t *target_client = NULL;
1336   char display_name_copy[MAX_DISPLAY_NAME_LEN];
1337   socket_t client_socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE; // Save socket for thread cleanup
1338 
1339   log_debug("SOCKET_DEBUG: Attempting to remove client %s", client_id);
1340   rwlock_wrlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1341 
1342   for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
1343     client_info_t *client = &g_client_manager.clients[i];
1344     if (strcmp(client->client_id, client_id) == 0 && client->client_id[0] != '\0') {
1345       // Check if already being removed by another thread
1346       // This prevents double-free and use-after-free crashes during concurrent cleanup
1347       if (atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down)) {
1348         rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1349         log_debug("Client %s already being removed by another thread, skipping", client_id);
1350         return 0; // Return success - removal is in progress
1351       }
1352       // Mark as shutting down and inactive immediately to stop new operations
1353       log_debug("Setting active=false in remove_client (client_id=%s, socket=%d)", client_id, client->socket);
1354       log_info("Removing client %s (socket=%d) - marking inactive and clearing video flags", client_id, client->socket);
1355       atomic_store_bool(&client->shutting_down, true);
1356       atomic_store_bool(&client->active, false);
1357       atomic_store_bool(&client->is_sending_video, false);
1358       atomic_store_bool(&client->is_sending_audio, false);
1359       target_client = client;
1360 
1361       // Store display name before clearing
1362       SAFE_STRNCPY(display_name_copy, client->display_name, MAX_DISPLAY_NAME_LEN - 1);
1363 
1364       // Save socket for tcp_server_stop_client_threads() before closing
1365       mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
1366       client_socket = client->socket; // Save socket for thread cleanup
1367       // NOTE: Do NOT call socket_shutdown() here - it sends FIN to peer and breaks the connection
1368       // The threads will exit naturally when they check shutting_down/active flags or hit errors
1369       mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
1370 
1371       // Shutdown packet queues to unblock send thread
1372       if (client->audio_queue) {
1373         packet_queue_stop(client->audio_queue);
1374       }
1375       // Video now uses double buffer, no queue to shutdown
1376 
1377       break;
1378     }
1379   }
1380 
1381   // If client not found, unlock and return
1382   if (!target_client) {
1383     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1384     log_warn("Cannot remove client %s: not found", client_id);
1385     return -1;
1386   }
1387 
1388   // Unregister client from session_host (for discovery mode support)
1389   // NOTE: Client may not be registered if crypto handshake failed before session_host registration
1390   if (server_ctx->session_host && target_client->session_client_id != 0) {
1391     asciichat_error_t session_result =
1392         session_host_remove_client(server_ctx->session_host, target_client->session_client_id);
1393     if (session_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1394       // ERROR_NOT_FOUND (91) is expected if client failed crypto before being registered with session_host
1395       if (session_result == ERROR_NOT_FOUND) {
1396         log_debug("Client %s not found in session_host (likely failed crypto before registration)", client_id);
1397       } else {
1398         log_warn("Failed to unregister client %s from session_host: %s", client_id,
1399                  asciichat_error_string(session_result));
1400       }
1401     } else {
1402       log_debug("Client %s unregistered from session_host", client_id);
1403     }
1404   }
1405 
1406   // Release write lock before joining threads.
1407   // This prevents deadlock with render threads that need read locks.
1408   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1409 
1410   // Phase 2: Stop all client threads
1411   // For TCP clients: use tcp_server thread pool management
1412   // For WebRTC clients: manually join threads (no socket-based thread pool)
1413   // Use is_tcp_client flag, not socket value - socket may already be INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE
1414   // even for TCP clients if it was closed earlier during cleanup.
1415   log_debug("Stopping all threads for client %s (socket %d, is_tcp=%d)", client_id, client_socket,
1416             target_client ? target_client->is_tcp_client : -1);
1417 
1418   if (target_client && target_client->is_tcp_client) {
1419     // TCP client: use tcp_server thread pool
1420     // This joins threads in stop_id order: receive(1), render(2), send(3)
1421     // Use saved client_socket for lookup (tcp_server needs original socket as key)
1422     if (client_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1423       asciichat_error_t stop_result = tcp_server_stop_client_threads(server_ctx->tcp_server, client_socket);
1424       if (stop_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1425         log_warn("Failed to stop threads for TCP client %s: error %d", client_id, stop_result);
1426         // Continue with cleanup even if thread stopping failed
1427       }
1428     } else {
1429       log_debug("TCP client %s socket already closed, threads should have already exited", client_id);
1430     }
1431   } else if (target_client) {
1432     // WebRTC client: manually join threads
1433     log_debug("Stopping WebRTC client %s threads (receive and send)", client_id);
1434 
1435     // Join receive thread (but skip if called from the receive thread itself to avoid deadlock)
1436     thread_id_t current_thread_id = asciichat_thread_self();
1437     if (asciichat_thread_equal(current_thread_id, target_client->receive_thread_id)) {
1438       log_debug("remove_client() called from receive thread for client %s, skipping self-join", client_id);
1439     } else {
1440       void *recv_result = NULL;
1441       asciichat_error_t recv_join_result = asciichat_thread_join(&target_client->receive_thread, &recv_result);
1442       if (recv_join_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1443         log_warn("Failed to join receive thread for WebRTC client %s: error %d", client_id, recv_join_result);
1444       } else {
1445         log_debug("Joined receive thread for WebRTC client %s", client_id);
1446       }
1447     }
1448 
1449     // Join dispatch thread (BEFORE destroying packet queues)
1450     if (asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&target_client->dispatch_thread)) {
1451       atomic_store_bool(&target_client->dispatch_thread_running, false);
1452       void *dispatch_result = NULL;
1453       asciichat_error_t dispatch_join_result = asciichat_thread_join(&target_client->dispatch_thread, &dispatch_result);
1454       if (dispatch_join_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1455         log_warn("Failed to join dispatch thread for WebRTC client %s: error %d", client_id, dispatch_join_result);
1456       } else {
1457         log_debug("Joined dispatch thread for WebRTC client %s", client_id);
1458       }
1459     }
1460 
1461     // Join send thread
1462     void *send_result = NULL;
1463     asciichat_error_t send_join_result = asciichat_thread_join(&target_client->send_thread, &send_result);
1464     if (send_join_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1465       log_warn("Failed to join send thread for WebRTC client %s: error %d", client_id, send_join_result);
1466     } else {
1467       log_debug("Joined send thread for WebRTC client %s", client_id);
1468     }
1469     // Note: Render threads still need to be stopped - they're created the same way for both TCP and WebRTC
1470     // For now, render threads are expected to exit when they check g_should_exit and client->active
1471   }
1472 
1473   // Destroy ACIP transport before closing socket
1474   // For WebSocket clients: LWS_CALLBACK_CLOSED already closed and destroyed the transport
1475   // Trying to destroy it again causes heap-use-after-free since LWS callbacks might still fire
1476   // For TCP clients: transport is ours to clean up
1477   if (target_client && target_client->transport && target_client->is_tcp_client) {
1478     acip_transport_destroy(target_client->transport);
1479     target_client->transport = NULL;
1480     log_debug("Destroyed ACIP transport for TCP client %s", client_id);
1481   } else if (target_client && target_client->transport && !target_client->is_tcp_client) {
1482     // WebSocket client - just NULL it out, LWS_CALLBACK_CLOSED already destroyed it
1483     target_client->transport = NULL;
1484     log_debug("Skipped transport destruction for WebSocket client %s (LWS already destroyed)", client_id);
1485   }
1486 
1487   // Now safe to close the socket (threads are stopped)
1488   if (client_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1489     log_debug("SOCKET_DEBUG: Closing socket %d for client %s after thread cleanup", client_socket, client_id);
1490     socket_close(client_socket);
1491   }
1492 
1493   // Phase 3: Clean up resources with write lock
1494   rwlock_wrlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1495 
1496   // Re-validate target_client pointer after reacquiring lock.
1497   // Another thread might have invalidated the pointer while we had the lock released.
1498   if (target_client) {
1499     // Verify client_id still matches and client is still in shutting_down state
1500     bool still_shutting_down = atomic_load_bool(&target_client->shutting_down);
1501     if (strcmp(target_client->client_id, client_id) != 0 || !still_shutting_down) {
1502       log_warn("Client %s pointer invalidated during thread cleanup (id=%s, shutting_down=%d)", client_id,
1503                target_client->client_id, still_shutting_down);
1504       rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1505       return 0; // Another thread completed the cleanup
1506     }
1507   }
1508 
1509   // Mark socket as closed in client structure
1510   if (target_client && target_client->socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1511     mutex_lock(&target_client->client_state_mutex);
1512     target_client->socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
1513     mutex_unlock(&target_client->client_state_mutex);
1514     log_debug("SOCKET_DEBUG: Client %s socket set to INVALID", target_client->client_id);
1515   }
1516 
1517   // Use the dedicated cleanup function to ensure all resources are freed
1518   cleanup_client_all_buffers(target_client);
1519 
1520   // Remove from audio mixer
1521   if (g_audio_mixer) {
1522     mixer_remove_source(g_audio_mixer, client_id);
1523 #ifdef DEBUG_AUDIO
1524     log_debug("Removed client %s from audio mixer", client_id);
1525 #endif
1526   }
1527 
1528   // Remove from uthash table
1529   // Verify client is actually in the hash table before deleting.
1530   // Another thread might have already removed it.
1531   if (target_client) {
1532     client_info_t *hash_entry = NULL;
1533     HASH_FIND_STR(g_client_manager.clients_by_id, target_client->client_id, hash_entry);
1534     if (hash_entry == target_client) {
1535       HASH_DELETE(hh, g_client_manager.clients_by_id, target_client);
1536       log_debug("Removed client %s from uthash table", client_id);
1537     } else {
1538       log_warn("Client %s already removed from hash table by another thread (found=%p, expected=%p)", client_id,
1539                (void *)hash_entry, (void *)target_client);
1540     }
1541   } else {
1542     log_warn("Failed to remove client %s from hash table (client not found)", client_id);
1543   }
1544 
1545   // Cleanup crypto context for this client
1546   if (target_client->crypto_initialized) {
1547     crypto_handshake_destroy(&target_client->crypto_handshake_ctx);
1548     target_client->crypto_initialized = false;
1549     log_debug("Crypto context cleaned up for client %s", client_id);
1550   }
1551 
1552   // Verify all threads have actually exited before resetting client_id.
1553   // Threads that are still starting (at RtlUserThreadStart) haven't checked client_id yet.
1554   // We must ensure threads are fully joined before zeroing the client struct.
1555   // Use exponential backoff for thread termination verification
1556   int retry_count = 0;
1557   const int max_retries = 5;
1558   while (retry_count < max_retries && (asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&target_client->send_thread) ||
1559                                        asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&target_client->receive_thread) ||
1560                                        asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&target_client->video_render_thread) ||
1561                                        asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&target_client->audio_render_thread))) {
1562     // Exponential backoff: 10ms, 20ms, 40ms, 80ms, 160ms
1563     uint32_t delay_ms = 10 * (1 << retry_count);
1564     log_warn("Client %s: Some threads still appear initialized (attempt %d/%d), waiting %ums", client_id,
1565              retry_count + 1, max_retries, delay_ms);
1566     platform_sleep_us(delay_ms * 1000);
1567     retry_count++;
1568   }
1569 
1570   if (retry_count == max_retries) {
1571     log_error("Client %s: Threads did not terminate after %d retries, proceeding with cleanup anyway", client_id,
1572               max_retries);
1573   }
1574 
1575   // Only reset client_id to 0 AFTER confirming threads are joined
1576   // This prevents threads that are starting from accessing a zeroed client struct
1577   // Reset client_id to NULL before destroying mutexes to prevent race conditions.
1578   // This ensures worker threads can detect shutdown and exit before the mutex is destroyed.
1579   // If we destroy the mutex first, threads might try to access a destroyed mutex.
1580   target_client->client_id[0] = '\0'; // Clear the client_id string
1581 
1582   // Wait for threads to observe the client_id reset
1583   // Use sufficient delay for memory visibility across all CPU cores
1584   platform_sleep_us(5 * US_PER_MS_INT); // 5ms delay for memory barrier propagation
1585 
1586   // Destroy mutexes and condition variables
1587   // IMPORTANT: Always destroy these even if threads didn't join properly
1588   // to prevent issues when the slot is reused
1589   mutex_destroy(&target_client->client_state_mutex);
1590   mutex_destroy(&target_client->send_mutex);
1591   cond_destroy(&target_client->dispatch_queue_cond);
1592 
1593   // Clear client structure
1594   // NOTE: After memset, the mutex handles are zeroed but the OS resources
1595   // have been released by the destroy calls above
1596   memset(target_client, 0, sizeof(client_info_t));
1597 
1598   // Recalculate client count
1599   int remaining_count = 0;
1600   for (int j = 0; j < MAX_CLIENTS; j++) {
1601     if (g_client_manager.clients[j].client_id[0] != '\0') {
1602       remaining_count++;
1603     }
1604   }
1605   g_client_manager.client_count = remaining_count;
1606 
1607   log_debug("Client removed: client_id=%s (%s) removed, remaining clients: %d", client_id, display_name_copy,
1608             remaining_count);
1609 
1610   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
1611 
1612   // Broadcast updated state
1613   broadcast_server_state_to_all_clients();
1614 
1615   return 0;
1616 }

int
  start_webrtc_client_threads (server_context_t * server_ctx, const char
  * client_id)


Start threads for a WebRTC client after crypto initialization.
    This is called by WebSocket handler after crypto handshake is initialized.
    It ensures receive thread doesn't try to process packets before crypto
    context exists.




Parameters


server_ctx Server context


client_id Client ID to start threads for


Returns


0 on success, -1 on failure

2676   }
2677

2679   if (!client) {
2680     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NOT_FOUND, "Client %s not found", client_id);
2681     return -1;
2682   }
2683 
2684   log_debug("Starting threads for WebRTC client %s...", client_id);
2685   return start_client_threads(server_ctx, client, false);
2686 }

void
  stop_client_threads (client_info_t * client)


Definition at line 2688 of file

2688                                                 {
2689   if (!client) {
2690     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Client is NULL");
2691     return;
2692   }
2693 
2694   // Signal threads to stop
2695   log_debug("Setting active=false in stop_client_threads (client_id=%s)", client->client_id);

2705   }

Variable
  Documentation




client_manager_t g_client_manager


Global client manager singleton - central coordination point.
    Global client manager.




This is the primary data structure for managing all connected
    clients. It serves as the bridge between main.c's connection accept loop and
    the per-client threading architecture.




STRUCTURE COMPONENTS:


    
  
  • clients[]: Array backing storage for client_info_t structs
    • 
  
  • client_hashtable: O(1) lookup table for client_id ->
    client_info_t*
    • 
  
  • client_count: Current number of active clients
    • 
  
  • mutex: Legacy mutex (mostly replaced by rwlock)
    • 
  
  • next_client_id: Monotonic counter for unique client identification
    • 



THREAD SAFETY: Protected by g_client_manager_rwlock for concurrent
    access


Definition at line 335 of file
  src/server/client.c.


Referenced by add_client(), add_webrtc_client(),
    any_clients_sending_video(),
    broadcast_server_state_to_all_clients(), find_client_by_id(),
    find_client_by_socket(), remove_client(),
    send_server_state_to_client(), server_main(),
    stats_logger_thread(), and update_server_stats().






rwlock_t g_client_manager_rwlock = {0}


Reader-writer lock protecting the global client manager.
    Read-write lock protecting client manager.




This lock enables high-performance concurrent access patterns:


    
  
  • Multiple threads can read client data simultaneously (stats,
    rendering)
    • 
  
  • Only one thread can modify client data at a time (add/remove
    operations)
    • 
  
  • Eliminates contention between read-heavy operations
    • 



USAGE PATTERN:


    
  
  • Read operations: rwlock_rdlock() for client lookups, stats
      gathering
    • 
  
  • Write operations: rwlock_wrlock() for add_client(),
      remove_client()
    • 
  
  • Always acquire THIS lock before per-client mutexes (lock ordering)
    • 



Definition at line 350 of file
  src/server/client.c.


350 {0};

    broadcast_server_state_to_all_clients(), find_client_by_id(),
    find_client_by_socket(), remove_client(),
    server_main(), stats_logger_thread(), and
    update_server_stats().
